Main application state and logic for the FORGE TUI.\n//!\n//! The `App` struct manages overall application state, view switching,\n//! and coordinates between different components.\n//!\n//! # Chat Backend Integration Architecture\n//!\n//! The chat functionality integrates an async `ChatBackend` into the synchronous\n//! TUI event loop using the following design:\n//!\n//! ## Async-to-Sync Bridge\n//!\n//! 1. **Tokio Runtime**: A single-threaded tokio runtime is created during App::new()\n//!    and lives for the duration of the application. This runtime handles all async\n//!    chat operations.\n//!\n//! 2. **Message Passing**: An `mpsc` channel connects async chat tasks to the sync UI:\n//!    - `chat_tx`: Sender held by App, used to submit chat requests\n//!    - `chat_rx`: Receiver held by a background task, processes requests\n//!    - `response_rx`: Receiver held by App, receives responses\n//!\n//! 3. **Non-Blocking Execution**: When user submits chat input:\n//!    - Send request to background task via `chat_tx`\n//!    - Set `chat_pending` flag and display loading indicator\n//!    - Continue UI event loop without blocking\n//!    - Poll `response_rx` each frame to check for responses\n//!\n//! ## State Management\n//!\n//! - `chat_pending: bool` - Whether a request is in flight\n//! - `chat_history: Vec<ChatExchange>` - Last 10 exchanges (user query + response)\n//! - `chat_backend: Option<ChatBackend>` - Initialized from config.yaml\n//!\n//! ## Error Handling\n//!\n//! - Backend initialization failures: App runs without chat (display error in UI)\n//! - Request timeout: Background task implements 30s timeout\n//! - API errors: Captured in response, displayed in chat history as error messages\n//!\n//! ## Performance\n//!\n//! - Background task prevents UI blocking\n//! - Response polling adds <1ms per frame\n//! - Chat history limited to 10 exchanges (~1KB memory)\n\nuse std::io;\nuse std::path::PathBuf;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::sync::mpsc::{self, Receiver, Sender, TryRecvError};\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse std::thread;\n\nuse forge_core::{UserSession, UserRole, SessionManager};\nuse forge_worker::{\n    LaunchConfig, SpawnRequest, WorkerLauncher,\n    discovery::DiscoveredWorker,\n};\nuse ratatui::{\n    Frame, Terminal,\n    backend::CrosstermBackend,\n    layout::{Constraint, Direction, Layout, Rect},\n    style::{Color, Modifier, Style},\n    text::{Line, Span, Text},\n    widgets::{Block, BorderType, Borders, Clear, ServerClientRequest::SpawnWorker { model, count } => {\n                    Some(ClientMessage::SpawnWorker { model, count })\n                }\n                ServerClientRequest::KillWorker { worker_id } => {\n                    Some(ClientMessage::KillWorker { worker_id })\n                }\n            };\n\n            if let Some(msg) = client_msg {\n                // Use send_direct to send the message (works because connect_and_run no longer consumes self)\n                if let Err(e) = client.send_direct(msg).await {\n                    warn!(\"Failed to send message to server: {}\", e);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// The inner event loop with frame-rate limiting and server client support.\n    fn run_loop_with_client(&mut self, terminal: &mut Terminal<CrosstermBackend<io::Stdout>>) -> AppResult<()> {\n        // Memory update interval (every 60 frames = ~1 second at 60fps)\n        let mut memory_update_counter: u32 = pending\n            if self.chat_pending {\n                self.chat_spinner_frame = (self.chat_spinner_frame + 1) % 4;\n                self.mark_dirty(); // Ensure UI updates for spinner animation\n            }\n\n            // Poll for server client messages FIRST - in client mode, this is critical\n            self.poll_server_client_messages();\n\n            // Poll for chat responses - this is non-blocking and must happen\n            // frequently to ensure responsive chat UX\n            self.poll_chat_responses();\n\n            // Update streaming display if active\n            self.update_streaming();\n\n            // Poll for config hot-reload changes\n            self.poll_config_changes();\n\n            // Optimized data polling - only poll if interval elapsed\n            let needs_poll = self.last_poll_time.elapsed() >= self.data_poll_interval;\n            if needs_poll {\n                let data_changed = self.data_manager.poll_updates();\n PathBuf::from(&forge_src).join(\"scripts/launchers/bead-worker-launcher.sh\"),\n            PathBuf::from(&home).join(\".forge/launchers/bead-worker-launcher.sh\"),\n            PathBuf::from(&forge_src).join(\"test/example-launchers/claude-code-launcher.sh\"),\n        ];\n\n        let launcher_path = launcher_paths\n            .into_iter()\n            .find(|p| p.exists())\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| {\n                // Fall back to first path even if it doesn't exist (will fail with clear error)\n                PathBuf::from(&forge_src).join(\"scripts/launchers/bead-worker-launcher.sh\")\n            });\n\n        // Generate a unique worker ID\n        let timestamp = chrono::Local::now().format(\"%Y%m%d-%H%M%S\");\n        let worker_id = format!(\"{}-{}-{:04}\", model, timestamp, rand::random::<u16>());\n        let session_name = format!(\"forge-{}\", worker_id);\n\n        // Use FORGE_WORKSPACE or default workspace\n        let workspace = std::env::var(\"FORGE_WORKSPACE\")\n            .map(PathBuf::from)\n            .unwrap_or_else(|_| PathBuf::from(&home).join(\"forge\"));\n\n        info!(\n            \"Spawning {} worker: id={}, session={}, workspace={}\",\n            executor.name(),\n            worker_id,\n            session_name,\n            workspace.display()\n        );\n\n        // Create launch config\n        let config = LaunchConfig::new(&launcher_path, &session_name, &workspace, model)\n            .with_tier(tier)\n            .with_timeout(60);\n\n        // Create spawn request\n        let request = SpawnRequest::new(&worker_id, config);\n\n        // Spawn the worker asynchronously using the runtime\n        let worker_id_clone = worker_id.clone();\n        let model_name = executor.name().to_string();\n        let result = self\n            .worker_runtime\n            .block_on(async { self.worker_launcher.spawn(request).await });\n\n        match result {\n            Ok(handle) => {\n                info!(\n                    \"Worker spawned successfully: {} (PID: {}, session: {})\",\n                    handle.id, handle.pid, handle.session_name\n                );\n                self.status_message = Some(format!(\n                    \"Spawned {} worker: {} (PID: {})\",\n                    model_name, worker_id_clone, handle.pid\n                ));\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\"Failed to spawn worker {}: {}\", worker_id_clone, e);\n                self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Failed to spawn {} worker: {}\", model_name, e));\n            }\n        }\n\n        self.mark_dirty();\n    }\n\n Provides keyboard input handling and event routing.\n4\t\n5\tuse crossterm::event::{KeyCode, KeyEvent, KeyModifiers};\n6\t\n7\tuse crate::view::View;\n8\t\n9\t/// Application-level events that can trigger state changes.\n10\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n11\tpub enum AppEvent {\n12\t    /// Switch to a specific view\n13\t    SwitchView(View),\n14\t    /// Cycle to the next view\n15\t    NextView,\n16\t    /// Cycle to the previous view\n17\t    PrevView,\n18\t    /// Show help overlay\n19\t    ShowHelp,\n20\t    /// Hide help overlay\n21\t    HideHelp,\n22\t    /// Request application quit\n23\t    Quit,\n24\t    /// Force quit (Ctrl+C)\n25\t    ForceQuit,\n26\t    /// Refresh current view\n27\t    Refresh,\n28\t    /// Cancel current operation\n29\t    Cancel,\n30\t    /// Navigate up in a list\n31\t    NavigateUp,\n32\t    /// Navigate down in a list\n33\t    NavigateDown,\n34\t    /// Page up\n35\t    PageUp,\n36\t    /// Page down\n37\t    PageDown,\n38\t    /// Go to top\n39\t    GoToTop,\n40\t    /// Go to bottom\n41\t    GoToBottom,\n42\t    /// Select/expand current item\n43\t    Select,\n44\t    /// Toggle item selection\n45\t    Toggle,\n46\t    /// Focus next panel\n47\t    FocusNext,\n48\t    /// Focus previous panel\n49\t    FocusPrev,\n50\t    /// Text input character\n51\t    TextInput(char),\n52\t    /// Backspace in text input\n53\t    Backspace,\n54\t    /// Submit text input\n55\t    Submit,\n56\t    /// Spawn a new worker (variant specifies executor type)\n57\t    SpawnWorker(WorkerExecutor),\n58\t    /// Kill selected worker\n59\t    KillWorker,\n60\t    /// Pause selected worker (Workers view only)\n61\t    PauseWorker,\n62\t    /// Pause all workers (Workers view only)\n63\t    PauseAllWorkers,\n64\t    /// Resume selected worker (Workers view only)\n65\t    ResumeWorker,\n66\t    /// Resume all workers (Workers view only)\n67\t    ResumeAllWorkers,\n68\t    /// Open configuration menu\n69\t    OpenConfig,\n70\t    /// Open budget configuration\n71\t    OpenBudgetConfig,\n72\t    /// Open worker configuration\n73\t    OpenWorkerConfig,\n74\t    /// Cycle to the next theme\n75\t    CycleTheme,\n76\t    /// Trigger forge update/rebuild and restart\n77\t    Update,\n78\t    /// Acknowledge the selected alert (Alerts view only)\n79\t    AcknowledgeAlert,\n80\t    /// Acknowledge all alerts (Alerts view only)\n81\t    AcknowledgeAllAlerts,\n82\t    /// No action needed\n83\t    None,\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\t/// Worker executor types for spawn actions.\n87\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n88\tpub enum WorkerExecutor {\n89\t    /// GLM-4.7 model\n90\t    Glm,\n91\t    /// Claude Sonnet 4.5\n92\t    Sonnet,\n93\t    /// Claude Opus 4.6\n94\t    Opus,\n95\t    /// Haiku model\n96\t    Haiku,\n97\t}\n98\t\n99\timpl WorkerExecutor {\n100\t    /// Returns the display name for this executor.\n101\t    pub fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n102\t        match self {\n103\t            WorkerExecutor::Glm => \"GLM-4.7\",\n104\t            WorkerExecutor::Sonnet => \"Sonnet 4.5\",\n105\t            WorkerExecutor::Opus => \"Opus 4.6\",\n106\t            WorkerExecutor::Haiku => \"Haiku\",\n107\t        }\n108\t    }\n109\t\n110\t    /// Returns the hotkey character for this executor.\n111\t    pub fn hotkey(&self) -> char {\n112\t        match self {\n113\t            WorkerExecutor::Glm => 'G',\n114\t            WorkerExecutor::Sonnet => 'S',\n115\t            WorkerExecutor::Opus => 'O',\n116\t            WorkerExecutor::Haiku => 'H',\n117\t        }\n118\t    }\n119\t}\n120\t\n121\t/// Input handler for converting key events to app events.\n122\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n123\tpub struct InputHandler {\n124\t    /// Whether we're currently in chat/text input mode\n125\t    chat_mode: bool,\n126\t    /// Current view for view-specific key handling\n127\t    current_view: View,\n128\t}\n129\t\n130\timpl InputHandler {\n131\t    /// Create a new input handler.\n132\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n133\t        Self {\n134\t            chat_mode: false,\n135\t            current_view: View::default(),\n136\t        }\n137\t    }\n138\t\n139\t    /// Set whether chat/text input mode is active.\n140\t    pub fn set_chat_mode(&mut self, active: bool) {\n141\t        self.chat_mode = active;\n142\t    }\n143\t\n144\t    /// Returns whether chat mode is active.\n145\t    pub fn is_chat_mode(&self) -> bool {\n146\t        self.chat_mode\n147\t    }\n148\t\n149\t    /// Set the current view for view-specific key handling.\n150\t    pub fn set_current_view(&mut self, view: View) {\n151\t        self.current_view = view;\n152\t    }\n153\t\n154\t    /// Returns the current view.\n155\t    pub fn current_view(&self) -> View {\n156\t        self.current_view\n157\t    }\n158\t\n159\t    /// Handle a key event and return the corresponding app event.\n160\t    pub fn handle_key(&mut self, key: KeyEvent) -> AppEvent {\n161\t        // Ctrl+C always force quits\n162\t        if key.modifiers.contains(KeyModifiers::CONTROL) && key.code == KeyCode::Char('c') {\n163\t            return AppEvent::ForceQuit;\n164\t        }\n165\t\n166\t        // Ctrl+L refreshes\n167\t        if key.modifiers.contains(KeyModifiers::CONTROL) && key.code == KeyCode::Char('l') {\n168\t            return AppEvent::Refresh;\n169\t        }\n170\t\n171\t        // Ctrl+U triggers update/rebuild\n172\t        if key.modifiers.contains(KeyModifiers::CONTROL) && key.code == KeyCode::Char('u') {\n173\t            return AppEvent::Update;\n174\t        }\n175\t\n176\t        // Escape cancels current operation or exits chat mode\n177\t        if key.code == KeyCode::Esc {\n178\t            if self.chat_mode {\n179\t                self.chat_mode = false;\n180\t                return AppEvent::Cancel;\n181\t            }\n182\t            return AppEvent::Cancel;\n183\t        }\n184\t\n185\t        // In chat mode, handle text input\n186\t        if self.chat_mode {\n187\t            return self.handle_chat_input(key);\n188\t        }\n189\t\n190\t        // Normal mode key handling\n191\t        self.handle_normal_mode(key)\n192\t    }\n193\t\n194\t    /// Handle input when in chat/text mode.\n195\t    fn handle_chat_input(&self, key: KeyEvent) -> AppEvent {\n196\t        match key.code {\n197\t            KeyCode::Enter => AppEvent::Submit,\n198\t            KeyCode::Backspace => AppEvent::Backspace,\n199\t            KeyCode::Char(c) => AppEvent::TextInput(c),\n200\t            KeyCode::Up => AppEvent::NavigateUp,\n201\t            KeyCode::Down => AppEvent::NavigateDown,\n202\t            _ => AppEvent::None,\n203\t        }\n204\t    }\n205\t\n206\t    /// Handle input when in normal navigation mode.\n207\t    fn handle_normal_mode(&mut self, key: KeyEvent) -> AppEvent {\n208\t        match key.code {\n209\t            // Quit\n210\t            KeyCode::Char('q') | KeyCode::Char('Q') => AppEvent::Quit,\n211\t\n212\t            // Help (when not used for spawn Haiku)\n213\t            KeyCode::Char('?') => AppEvent::ShowHelp,\n214\t            // Note: 'h' is now spawn Haiku, help only via '?'\n215\t\n216\t            // Quick Actions - Spawn workers\n217\t            KeyCode::Char('g') | KeyCode::Char('G') => AppEvent::SpawnWorker(WorkerExecutor::Glm),\n218\t            KeyCode::Char('s') | KeyCode::Char('S') => {\n219\t                AppEvent::SpawnWorker(WorkerExecutor::Sonnet)\n220\t            }\n221\t            KeyCode::Char('o') => AppEvent::SpawnWorker(WorkerExecutor::Opus), // lowercase only\n222\t            KeyCode::Char('h') => AppEvent::SpawnWorker(WorkerExecutor::Haiku),\n223\t\n224\t            // Quick Actions - Kill worker\n225\t            KeyCode::Char('k') => AppEvent::KillWorker,\n226\t\n227\t            // Workers view: Pause/Resume controls\n228\t            // 'p' = pause selected worker (in Workers view), 'P' = pause all workers\n229\t            // 'r' = resume selected worker, 'R' = resume all workers\n230\t            KeyCode::Char('p') if self.current_view == View::Workers => AppEvent::PauseWorker,\n231\t            KeyCode::Char('p') if self.current_view != View::Workers => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Perf),\n232\t            KeyCode::Char('P') if self.current_view == View::Workers => AppEvent::PauseAllWorkers,\n233\t            KeyCode::Char('r') if self.current_view == View::Workers => AppEvent::ResumeWorker,\n234\t            KeyCode::Char('R') if self.current_view == View::Workers => AppEvent::ResumeAllWorkers,\n235\t\n236\t            // Routing view (lowercase 'r' when not in Workers view)\n237\t            KeyCode::Char('r') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Routing),\n238\t\n239\t            // Subscriptions view (lowercase 'u')\n240\t            KeyCode::Char('u') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Subscriptions),\n241\t\n242\t            // View navigation hotkeys (also quick actions - view shortcuts)\n243\t            KeyCode::Char('w') | KeyCode::Char('W') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Workers),\n244\t            KeyCode::Char('t') | KeyCode::Char('T') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Tasks),\n245\t            // 'a' for Alerts view, 'A' for acknowledge all when in Alerts view\n246\t            KeyCode::Char('a') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Alerts),\n247\t            KeyCode::Char('A') if self.current_view == View::Alerts => AppEvent::AcknowledgeAllAlerts,\n248\t            KeyCode::Char('A') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Alerts),\n249\t            // 'l' or 'L' for Logs view\n250\t            KeyCode::Char('l') | KeyCode::Char('L') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Logs),\n251\t\n252\t            // Quick Actions - Configure\n253\t            KeyCode::Char('M') => AppEvent::OpenConfig,\n254\t            KeyCode::Char('b') | KeyCode::Char('B') => AppEvent::OpenBudgetConfig,\n255\t            // Note: 'c' lowercase is Costs view, uppercase is now CycleTheme\n256\t            // Worker config moved to 'U' (uppercase U)\n257\t            KeyCode::Char('U') => AppEvent::OpenWorkerConfig,\n258\t            // Cycle theme (uppercase C)\n259\t            KeyCode::Char('C') => AppEvent::CycleTheme,\n260\t\n261\t            // Costs view (only lowercase)\n262\t            KeyCode::Char('c') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Costs),\n263\t\n264\t            // Metrics view (lowercase, uppercase is OpenConfig)\n265\t            KeyCode::Char('m') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Metrics),\n266\t\n267\t            // Overview view\n268\t            KeyCode::Char('O') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Overview),\n269\t\n270\t            // Chat mode activation\n271\t            KeyCode::Char(':') => {\n272\t                self.chat_mode = true;\n273\t                AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Chat)\n274\t            }\n275\t\n276\t            // Tab cycling\n277\t            KeyCode::Tab => {\n278\t                if key.modifiers.contains(KeyModifiers::SHIFT) {\n279\t                    AppEvent::PrevView\n280\t                } else {\n281\t                    AppEvent::NextView\n282\t                }\n283\t            }\n284\t            KeyCode::BackTab => AppEvent::PrevView,\n285\t\n286\t            // List navigation (use 'j' for down since 'k' is unused now)\n287\t            KeyCode::Up => AppEvent::NavigateUp,\n288\t            KeyCode::Down | KeyCode::Char('j') => AppEvent::NavigateDown,\n289\t            KeyCode::PageUp => AppEvent::PageUp,\n290\t            KeyCode::PageDown => AppEvent::PageDown,\n291\t            KeyCode::Home => AppEvent::GoToTop,\n292\t            KeyCode::End => AppEvent::GoToBottom,\n293\t\n294\t            // Selection\n295\t            KeyCode::Enter => AppEvent::Select,\n296\t            KeyCode::Char(' ') => AppEvent::Toggle,\n297\t\n298\t            // Alerts view: Acknowledge all alerts with 'A' (already mapped to SwitchView)\n299\t            // We handle 'A' in the Alerts view via app.rs instead\n300\t\n301\t            _ => AppEvent::None,\n302\t        }\n303\t    }\n304\t}\n305\t\n306\t#[cfg(test)]\n307\tmod tests {\n308\t    use super::*;\n309\t\n310\t    fn key_event(code: KeyCode) -> KeyEvent {\n311\t        KeyEvent::new(code, KeyModifiers::NONE)\n312\t    }\n313\t\n314\t    fn key_event_with_mods(code: KeyCode, mods: KeyModifiers) -> KeyEvent {\n315\t        KeyEvent::new(code, mods)\n316\t    }\n317\t\n318\t    #[test]\n319\t    fn test_view_hotkeys() {\n320\t        let mut handler = InputHandler::new();\n321\t\n322\t        // 'O' uppercase for Overview (lowercase 'o' is spawn Opus)\n323\t        assert_eq!(\n324\t            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('O'))),\n325\t            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Overview)\n326\t        );\n327\t        assert_eq!(\n328\t            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('w'))),\n329\t            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Workers)\n330\t        );\n331\t        assert_eq!(\n332\t            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('t'))),\n333\t            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Tasks)\n334\t        );\n335\t        assert_eq!(\n336\t            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('c'))),\n337\t            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Costs)\n338\t        );\n339\t        assert_eq!(\n340\t            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('m'))),\n341\t            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Metrics)\n342\t        );\n343\t        assert_eq!(\n344\t handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('L'))),\n355\t            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Logs)\n356\t        );\n357\t    }\n358\t\n359\t    #[test]\n360\t    fn test_chat_mode_activation() test_pause_resume_keys_in_workers_view() {\n522\t        let mut handler = InputHandler::new();\n523\t\n524\t        // Set to Workers view\n525\t assert_eq!(\n535\t            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('P'))),\n536\t            AppEvent::PauseAllWorkers\n537\t        );\n538\t\n539\t        // 'r' in Workers view should resume selected worker\n540\t        assert_eq!(\n541\t            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('r'))),\n542\t            AppEvent::ResumeWorker\n543\t        );\n544\t\n545\t        // 'R' (Shift+r) in Workers view should resume all workers\n546\t        assert_eq!(\n547\t            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('R'))),\n548\t            AppEvent::ResumeAllWorkers\n549\t        );\n550\t    }\n551\t\n552\t    #[test]\n553\t    fn test_pause_resume_keys_not_in_workers_view() {\n554\t        let mut handler = InputHandler::new();\n555\t\n556\t        // Default view is Overview, 'p' should switch to Perf view\n557\t        assert_eq!(\n558\t            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('p'))),\n559\t            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Perf)\n560\t        );\n561\t\n562\t        // 'r' in non-Workers view should switch to Routing view\n563\t        assert_eq!(\n564\t            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('r'))),\n565\t            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Routing)\n566\t        );\n567\t\n568\t        // 'P' and 'R' should do nothing in non-Workers view\n569\t        assert_eq!(\n570\t handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('R'))),\n575\t            AppEvent::None\n576\t        );\n577\t    }\n578\t}\n579\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c858a464-6fd4-481a-85a6-5f61e68cd76f","uuid":"bd1391c3-5180-4241-991e-897446f49e4c","timestamp":"2026-05-22T22:52:57.641Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/FORGE/crates/forge-tui/src/event.rs","content":"//! Event handling for the FORGE TUI.\n//!\n//! Provides keyboard input handling and event routing.\n\nuse crossterm::event::{KeyCode, KeyEvent, KeyModifiers};\n\nuse crate::view::View;\n\n/// Application-level events that can trigger state changes.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum AppEvent {\n    /// Switch to a specific view\n    SwitchView(View),\n    /// Cycle to the next view\n    NextView,\n    /// Cycle to the previous view\n    PrevView,\n    /// Show help overlay\n    ShowHelp,\n    /// Hide help overlay\n    HideHelp,\n    /// Request application quit\n    Quit,\n    /// Force quit (Ctrl+C)\n    ForceQuit,\n    /// Refresh current view\n    Refresh,\n    /// Cancel current operation\n    Cancel,\n    /// Navigate up in a list\n    NavigateUp,\n    /// Navigate down in a list\n    NavigateDown,\n    /// Page up\n    PageUp,\n    /// Page down\n    PageDown,\n    /// Go to top\n    GoToTop,\n    /// Go to bottom\n    GoToBottom,\n    /// Select/expand current item\n    Select,\n    /// Toggle item selection\n    Toggle,\n    /// Focus next panel\n    FocusNext,\n    /// Focus previous panel\n    FocusPrev,\n    /// Text input character\n    TextInput(char),\n    /// Backspace in text input\n    Backspace,\n    /// Submit text input\n    Submit,\n    /// Spawn a new worker (variant specifies executor type)\n    SpawnWorker(WorkerExecutor),\n    /// Kill selected worker\n    KillWorker,\n    /// Pause selected worker (Workers view only)\n    PauseWorker,\n    /// Pause all workers (Workers view only)\n    PauseAllWorkers,\n    /// Resume selected worker (Workers view only)\n    ResumeWorker,\n    /// Resume all workers (Workers view only)\n    ResumeAllWorkers,\n    /// Open configuration menu\n    OpenConfig,\n    /// Open budget configuration\n    OpenBudgetConfig,\n    /// Open worker configuration\n    OpenWorkerConfig,\n    /// Cycle to the next theme\n    CycleTheme,\n    /// Trigger forge update/rebuild and restart\n    Update,\n    /// Acknowledge the selected alert (Alerts view only)\n    AcknowledgeAlert,\n    /// Acknowledge all alerts (Alerts view only)\n    AcknowledgeAllAlerts,\n    /// No action needed\n    None,\n}\n\n/// Worker executor types for spawn actions.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum WorkerExecutor {\n    /// GLM-4.7 model\n    Glm,\n    /// Claude Sonnet 4.5\n    Sonnet,\n    /// Claude Opus 4.6\n    Opus,\n    /// Haiku model\n    Haiku,\n}\n\nimpl WorkerExecutor {\n    /// Returns the display name for this executor.\n    pub fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            WorkerExecutor::Glm => \"GLM-4.7\",\n            WorkerExecutor::Sonnet => \"Sonnet 4.5\",\n            WorkerExecutor::Opus => \"Opus 4.6\",\n            WorkerExecutor::Haiku => \"Haiku\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the hotkey character for this executor.\n    pub fn hotkey(&self) -> char {\n        match self {\n            WorkerExecutor::Glm => 'G',\n            WorkerExecutor::Sonnet => 'S',\n            WorkerExecutor::Opus => 'O',\n            WorkerExecutor::Haiku => 'H',\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Input handler for converting key events to app events.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\npub struct InputHandler {\n    /// Whether we're currently in chat/text input mode\n    chat_mode: bool,\n    /// Current view for view-specific key handling\n    current_view: View,\n}\n\nimpl InputHandler {\n    /// Create a new input handler.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            chat_mode: false,\n            current_view: View::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set whether chat/text input mode is active.\n    pub fn set_chat_mode(&mut self, active: bool) {\n        self.chat_mode = active;\n    }\n\n    /// Returns whether chat mode is active.\n    pub fn is_chat_mode(&self) -> bool {\n        self.chat_mode\n    }\n\n    /// Set the current view for view-specific key handling.\n    pub fn set_current_view(&mut self, view: View) {\n        self.current_view = view;\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the current view.\n    pub fn current_view(&self) -> View {\n        self.current_view\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a key event and return the corresponding app event.\n    pub fn handle_key(&mut self, key: KeyEvent) -> AppEvent {\n        // Ctrl+C always force quits\n        if key.modifiers.contains(KeyModifiers::CONTROL) && key.code == KeyCode::Char('c') {\n            return AppEvent::ForceQuit;\n        }\n\n        // Ctrl+L refreshes\n        if key.modifiers.contains(KeyModifiers::CONTROL) && key.code == KeyCode::Char('l') {\n            return AppEvent::Refresh;\n        }\n\n        // Ctrl+U triggers update/rebuild\n        if key.modifiers.contains(KeyModifiers::CONTROL) && key.code == KeyCode::Char('u') {\n            return AppEvent::Update;\n        }\n\n        // Escape cancels current operation or exits chat mode\n        if key.code == KeyCode::Esc {\n            if self.chat_mode {\n                self.chat_mode = false;\n                return AppEvent::Cancel;\n            }\n            return AppEvent::Cancel;\n        }\n\n        // In chat mode, handle text input\n        if self.chat_mode {\n            return self.handle_chat_input(key);\n        }\n\n        // Normal mode key handling\n        self.handle_normal_mode(key)\n    }\n\n    /// Handle input when in chat/text mode.\n    fn handle_chat_input(&self, key: KeyEvent) -> AppEvent {\n        match key.code {\n            KeyCode::Enter => AppEvent::Submit,\n            KeyCode::Backspace => AppEvent::Backspace,\n            KeyCode::Char(c) => AppEvent::TextInput(c),\n            KeyCode::Up => AppEvent::NavigateUp,\n            KeyCode::Down => AppEvent::NavigateDown,\n            _ => AppEvent::None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Handle input when in normal navigation mode.\n    fn handle_normal_mode(&mut self, key: KeyEvent) -> AppEvent {\n        match key.code {\n            // Quit\n            KeyCode::Char('q') | KeyCode::Char('Q') => AppEvent::Quit,\n\n            // Help (when not used for spawn Haiku)\n            KeyCode::Char('?') => AppEvent::ShowHelp,\n            // Note: 'h' is now spawn Haiku, help only via '?'\n\n            // Quick Actions - Spawn workers\n            KeyCode::Char('g') | KeyCode::Char('G') => AppEvent::SpawnWorker(WorkerExecutor::Glm),\n            KeyCode::Char('s') | KeyCode::Char('S') => {\n                AppEvent::SpawnWorker(WorkerExecutor::Sonnet)\n            }\n            KeyCode::Char('o') => AppEvent::SpawnWorker(WorkerExecutor::Opus), // lowercase only\n            KeyCode::Char('h') => AppEvent::SpawnWorker(WorkerExecutor::Haiku),\n\n            // Quick Actions - Kill worker\n            KeyCode::Char('k') => AppEvent::KillWorker,\n\n            // Workers view: Pause/Resume controls\n            // 'p' = pause selected worker (in Workers view), 'P' = pause all workers\n            // 'r' = resume selected worker, 'R' = resume all workers\n            KeyCode::Char('p') if self.current_view == View::Workers => AppEvent::PauseWorker,\n            KeyCode::Char('p') if self.current_view != View::Workers => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Perf),\n            KeyCode::Char('P') if self.current_view == View::Workers => AppEvent::PauseAllWorkers,\n            KeyCode::Char('r') if self.current_view == View::Workers => AppEvent::ResumeWorker,\n            KeyCode::Char('R') if self.current_view == View::Workers => AppEvent::ResumeAllWorkers,\n\n            // Routing view (lowercase 'r' when not in Workers view)\n            KeyCode::Char('r') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Routing),\n\n            // Subscriptions view (lowercase 'u')\n            KeyCode::Char('u') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Subscriptions),\n\n            // View navigation hotkeys (also quick actions - view shortcuts)\n            KeyCode::Char('w') | KeyCode::Char('W') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Workers),\n            KeyCode::Char('t') | KeyCode::Char('T') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Tasks),\n            // 'a' for Alerts view, 'A' for acknowledge all when in Alerts view\n            KeyCode::Char('a') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Alerts),\n            KeyCode::Char('A') if self.current_view == View::Alerts => AppEvent::AcknowledgeAllAlerts,\n            KeyCode::Char('A') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Alerts),\n            // 'l' or 'L' for Logs view\n            KeyCode::Char('l') | KeyCode::Char('L') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Logs),\n\n            // Quick Actions - Configure\n            KeyCode::Char('M') => AppEvent::OpenConfig,\n            KeyCode::Char('b') | KeyCode::Char('B') => AppEvent::OpenBudgetConfig,\n            // Note: 'c' lowercase is Costs view, uppercase is now CycleTheme\n            // Worker config moved to 'U' (uppercase U)\n            KeyCode::Char('U') => AppEvent::OpenWorkerConfig,\n            // Cycle theme (uppercase C)\n            KeyCode::Char('C') => AppEvent::CycleTheme,\n\n            // Costs view (only lowercase)\n            KeyCode::Char('c') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Costs),\n\n            // Metrics view (lowercase, uppercase is OpenConfig)\n            KeyCode::Char('m') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Metrics),\n\n            // Overview view\n            KeyCode::Char('O') => AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Overview),\n\n            // Chat mode activation\n            KeyCode::Char(':') => {\n                self.chat_mode = true;\n                AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Chat)\n            }\n\n            // Tab cycling\n            KeyCode::Tab => {\n                if key.modifiers.contains(KeyModifiers::SHIFT) {\n                    AppEvent::PrevView\n                } else {\n                    AppEvent::NextView\n                }\n            }\n            KeyCode::BackTab => AppEvent::PrevView,\n\n            // List navigation (use 'j' for down since 'k' is unused now)\n            KeyCode::Up => AppEvent::NavigateUp,\n            KeyCode::Down | KeyCode::Char('j') => AppEvent::NavigateDown,\n            KeyCode::PageUp => AppEvent::PageUp,\n            KeyCode::PageDown => AppEvent::PageDown,\n            KeyCode::Home => AppEvent::GoToTop,\n            KeyCode::End => AppEvent::GoToBottom,\n\n            // Selection\n            KeyCode::Enter => AppEvent::Select,\n            KeyCode::Char(' ') => AppEvent::Toggle,\n\n            // Alerts view: Acknowledge all alerts with 'A' (already mapped to SwitchView)\n            // We handle 'A' in the Alerts view via app.rs instead\n\n            _ => AppEvent::None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    fn key_event(code: KeyCode) -> KeyEvent {\n        KeyEvent::new(code, KeyModifiers::NONE)\n    }\n\n    fn key_event_with_mods(code: KeyCode, mods: KeyModifiers) -> KeyEvent {\n        KeyEvent::new(code, mods)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_view_hotkeys() {\n        let mut handler = InputHandler::new();\n\n        // 'O' uppercase for Overview (lowercase 'o' is spawn Opus)\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('O'))),\n            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Overview)\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('w'))),\n            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Workers)\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('t'))),\n            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Tasks)\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('c'))),\n            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Costs)\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('m'))),\n            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Metrics)\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('l'))),\n            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Logs)\n        );\n        // 'a' for Alerts view\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('a'))),\n            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Alerts)\n        );\n        // 'L' for Logs view\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('L'))),\n            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Logs)\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_chat_mode_activation() {\n        let mut handler = InputHandler::new();\n        assert!(!handler.is_chat_mode());\n\n        let event = handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char(':')));\n        assert_eq!(event, AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Chat));\n        assert!(handler.is_chat_mode());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_chat_mode_escape() {\n        let mut handler = InputHandler::new();\n        handler.set_chat_mode(true);\n        assert!(handler.is_chat_mode());\n\n        let event = handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Esc));\n        assert_eq!(event, AppEvent::Cancel);\n        assert!(!handler.is_chat_mode());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_chat_mode_input() {\n        let mut handler = InputHandler::new();\n        handler.set_chat_mode(true);\n\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('a'))),\n            AppEvent::TextInput('a')\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Backspace)),\n            AppEvent::Backspace\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Enter)),\n            AppEvent::Submit\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_ctrl_c_force_quit() {\n        let mut handler = InputHandler::new();\n\n        // Works in normal mode\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event_with_mods(\n                KeyCode::Char('c'),\n                KeyModifiers::CONTROL\n            )),\n            AppEvent::ForceQuit\n        );\n\n        // Also works in chat mode\n        handler.set_chat_mode(true);\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event_with_mods(\n                KeyCode::Char('c'),\n                KeyModifiers::CONTROL\n            )),\n            AppEvent::ForceQuit\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_tab_cycling() {\n        let mut handler = InputHandler::new();\n\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Tab)),\n            AppEvent::NextView\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event_with_mods(KeyCode::Tab, KeyModifiers::SHIFT)),\n            AppEvent::PrevView\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::BackTab)),\n            AppEvent::PrevView\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_navigation_keys() {\n        let mut handler = InputHandler::new();\n\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Up)),\n            AppEvent::NavigateUp\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Down)),\n            AppEvent::NavigateDown\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('j'))),\n            AppEvent::NavigateDown\n        );\n        // 'k' is now KillWorker, not NavigateUp\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('k'))),\n            AppEvent::KillWorker\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_help_and_quit() {\n        let mut handler = InputHandler::new();\n\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('?'))),\n            AppEvent::ShowHelp\n        );\n        // 'h' is now spawn Haiku, help only via '?'\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('h'))),\n            AppEvent::SpawnWorker(WorkerExecutor::Haiku)\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('q'))),\n            AppEvent::Quit\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_case_insensitive_hotkeys() {\n        let mut handler = InputHandler::new();\n\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('W'))),\n            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Workers)\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('Q'))),\n            AppEvent::Quit\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_theme_cycle_hotkey() {\n        let mut handler = InputHandler::new();\n\n        // Uppercase 'C' cycles theme\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('C'))),\n            AppEvent::CycleTheme\n        );\n\n        // Lowercase 'c' is still Costs view\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('c'))),\n            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Costs)\n        );\n\n        // 'U' is now OpenWorkerConfig (was 'C')\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('U'))),\n            AppEvent::OpenWorkerConfig\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_pause_resume_keys_in_workers_view() {\n        let mut handler = InputHandler::new();\n\n        // Set to Workers view\n        handler.set_current_view(View::Workers);\n\n        // 'p' in Workers view should pause selected worker\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('p'))),\n            AppEvent::PauseWorker\n        );\n\n        // 'P' (Shift+p) in Workers view should pause all workers\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('P'))),\n            AppEvent::PauseAllWorkers\n        );\n\n        // 'r' in Workers view should resume selected worker\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('r'))),\n            AppEvent::ResumeWorker\n        );\n\n        // 'R' (Shift+r) in Workers view should resume all workers\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('R'))),\n            AppEvent::ResumeAllWorkers\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_pause_resume_keys_not_in_workers_view() {\n        let mut handler = InputHandler::new();\n\n        // Default view is Overview, 'p' should switch to Perf view\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('p'))),\n            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Perf)\n        );\n\n        // 'r' in non-Workers view should switch to Routing view\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('r'))),\n            AppEvent::SwitchView(View::Routing)\n        );\n\n        // 'P' and 'R' should do nothing in non-Workers view\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('P'))),\n            AppEvent::None\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            handler.handle_key(key_event(KeyCode::Char('R'))),\n            AppEvent::None\n        );\n    }\n}\n","numLines":579,"startLine":1,"totalLines":579}}}

Main application state and logic for the FORGE TUI.\n//!\n//! The `App` struct manages overall application state, view switching,\n//! and coordinates between different components.\n//!\n//! # Chat Backend Integration Architecture\n//!\n//! The chat functionality integrates an async `ChatBackend` into the synchronous\n//! TUI event loop using the following design:\n//!\n//! ## Async-to-Sync Bridge\n//!\n//! 1. **Tokio Runtime**: A single-threaded tokio runtime is created during App::new()\n//!    and lives for the duration of the application. This runtime handles all async\n//!    chat operations.\n//!\n//! 2. **Message Passing**: An `mpsc` channel connects async chat tasks to the sync UI:\n//!    - `chat_tx`: Sender held by App, used to submit chat requests\n//!    - `chat_rx`: Receiver held by a background task, processes requests\n//!    - `response_rx`: Receiver held by App, receives responses\n//!\n//! 3. **Non-Blocking Execution**: When user submits chat input:\n//!    - Send request to background task via `chat_tx`\n//!    - Set `chat_pending` flag and display loading indicator\n//!    - Continue UI event loop without blocking\n//!    - Poll `response_rx` each frame to check for responses\n//!\n//! ## State Management\n//!\n//! - `chat_pending: bool` - Whether a request is in flight\n//! - `chat_history: Vec<ChatExchange>` - Last 10 exchanges (user query + response)\n//! - `chat_backend: Option<ChatBackend>` - Initialized from config.yaml\n//!\n//! ## Error Handling\n//!\n//! - Backend initialization failures: App runs without chat (display error in UI)\n//! - Request timeout: Background task implements 30s timeout\n//! - API errors: Captured in response, displayed in chat history as error messages\n//!\n//! ## Performance\n//!\n//! - Background task prevents UI blocking\n//! - Response polling adds <1ms per frame\n//! - Chat history limited to 10 exchanges (~1KB memory)\n\nuse std::io;\nuse std::path::PathBuf;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::sync::mpsc::{self, Receiver, Sender, TryRecvError};\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse std::thread;\n\nuse crossterm::event::{self, Event, KeyEvent};\nuse forge_config::ForgeConfig;\nuse forge_chat::{ChatBackend, ChatConfig, ChatResponse, StreamingChatChunk};\nuse forge_core::types::WorkerTier;\nuse forge_core::{UserSession, UserRole, SessionManager};\nuse forge_worker::{\n    LaunchConfig, SpawnRequest, WorkerLauncher,\n    discovery::DiscoveredWorker,\n};\nuse ratatui::{\n    Frame, Terminal,\n    backend::CrosstermBackend,\n    layout::{Constraint, Direction, Layout, Rect},\n    style::{Color, Modifier, Style},\n    text::{Line, Span, Text},\n    widgets::{Block, BorderType, Borders, Clear, Paragraph, Wrap},\n};\n\nuse crate::config_menu::{ConfigMenuType, build_budget_items, build_settings_items, build_worker_items, draw_config_menu};\nuse crate::config_watcher::{ConfigEvent, ConfigWatcher};\nuse crate::cost_panel::CostPanel;\nuse crate::data::DataManager;\nuse crate::event::{AppEvent, InputHandler};\nuse crate::metrics_panel::MetricsPanel;\nuse crate::perf_panel::PerfPanel;\nuse crate::theme::ThemeManager;\nuse crate::view::{FocusPanel, LayoutMode, View};\nuse crate::widget::QuickActionsPanel;\nuse crate::error_recovery::{ErrorCategory, ErrorSeverity, SharedErrorRecoveryManager};\nuse tracing::{error, info, warn};\n\n/// Result type for app operations.\npub type AppResult<T> = std::result::Result<T, Box<dyn std::error::Error>>;\n\n/// Target frame rate (60 FPS = ~16.67ms per frame).\nconst TARGET_FPS: u64 = 60;\nconst FRAME_DURATION: Duration = Duration::from_millis(1000 / TARGET_FPS);\n\n/// Default data polling interval (100ms).\nconst DEFAULT_DATA_POLL_INTERVAL_MS: u64 = 100;\n\n/// Header timestamp cache duration (update every second).\nconst TIMESTAMP_CACHE_DURATION: Duration = Duration::from_secs(1);\n\n/// A pending action that requires user confirmation before execution.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\npub enum PendingAction {\n    /// Spawn a new worker with the specified executor\n    SpawnWorker(crate::event::WorkerExecutor),\n    /// Kill a specific worker by suffix\n    KillWorker { suffix: String, worker_type: String },\n    /// Pause a specific worker\n    PauseWorker { worker_id: String },\n    /// Pause all workers\n    PauseAllWorkers { count: usize },\n    /// Resume a specific worker\n    ResumeWorker { worker_id: String },\n    /// Resume all workers\n    ResumeAllWorkers { count: usize },\n}\n\n/// Request message to the server client background task.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\npub enum ServerClientRequest {\n    /// Assign a bead to a user\n    AssignBead { bead_id: String, to: String },\n    /// Unassign a bead\n    UnassignBead { bead_id: String },\n    /// Send chat message to server\n    SendChat { message: String },\n    /// Update current view\n    UpdateView { view: String },\n    /// Spawn worker (if permitted)\n    SpawnWorker { model: String, count: u32 },\n    /// Kill worker (if permitted)\n    KillWorker { worker_id: String },\n}\n\n/// Message from the server client background task to the UI.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\npub enum ServerClientMessage {\n    /// Connected and authenticated successfully\n    Connected { session: forge_core::UserSession, server_url: String },\n    /// State update received from server\n    StateUpdate { workers: Vec<forge_server::protocol::WorkerState>, beads: Vec<forge_server::protocol::BeadState> },\n    /// User joined the session\n    UserJoined { user: String, display_name: String, role: forge_core::UserRole },\n    /// User left the session\n    UserLeft { user: String },\n    /// Bead assigned notification\n    BeadAssigned { bead_id: String, assigned_to: String, assigned_by: String },\n    /// Chat message from another user\n    ChatMessage { from: String, message: String, timestamp: chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc> },\n    /// Connection error\n    ConnectionError { message: String },\n    /// Disconnected from server\n    Disconnected,\n}\n\n/// Configuration for connecting to a FORGE server as a client.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\npub struct ClientConfig {\n    /// Server WebSocket URL (e.g., ws://localhost:8080/ws)\n display.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\nstruct PendingChatExchange {\n    query: String,\n    response_text: String,\n    timestamp: String,\n    tool_calls: Vec<ToolCallInfo>,\n    side_effects: Vec<SideEffectInfo>,\n    confirmation: Option<ConfirmationInfo>,\n    metadata: ResponseMetadata,\n}\n\n/// A single chat exchange (user query + assistant response).\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub struct ChatExchange {\n    pub user_query: String,\n    pub assistant_response: String,\n    pub timestamp: String,\n    pub is_error: bool,\n    /// Tool calls made during this exchange\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub tool_calls: Vec<ToolCallInfo>,\n    /// Side effects from tool execution\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub side_effects: Vec<SideEffectInfo>,\n    /// Confirmation prompt if action requires approval\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub confirmation: Option<ConfirmationInfo>,\n    /// Response metadata (duration, cost, provider)\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub metadata: ResponseMetadata,\n    /// Actionable guidance for errors (how to fix)\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub error_guidance: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Information about a tool call for display.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub struct ToolCallInfo {\n    /// Tool name (e.g., \"spawn_worker\", \"get_worker_status\")\n    pub name: String,\n    /// Whether the tool execution succeeded\n    pub success: bool,\n    /// Human-readable result message\n    pub message: String,\n}\n\n/// Information about a side effect for display.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub struct SideEffectInfo {\n    /// Type of effect (e.g., \"spawn\", \"kill\", \"assign\")\n    pub effect_type: String,\n    /// Description of what happened\n    pub description: String,\n}\n\n/// Confirmation prompt information for display.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub struct ConfirmationInfo {\n    /// Title of the confirmation\n    pub title: String,\n    /// Description of what will happen\n    pub description: String,\n    /// Warning level (info, warning, danger)\n    pub level: ConfirmationLevel,\n    /// Estimated cost impact\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub cost_impact: Option<f64>,\n    /// Items that will be affected\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub affected_items: Vec<String>,\n    /// Whether this action is reversible\n    pub reversible: bool,\n}\n\n/// Warning level for confirmation prompts.\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub enum ConfirmationLevel {\n    /// Informational (no danger)\n    Info,\n    /// Warning (some risk)\n    Warning,\n    /// Danger (high risk, destructive)\n    Danger,\n}\n\n/// Response metadata for display.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]\npub struct ResponseMetadata {\n    /// Duration in milliseconds\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub duration_ms: u64,\n    /// Estimated cost in USD\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub cost_usd: Option<f64>,\n    /// Provider name\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub provider: String,\n}\n\n/// Generate actionable guidance for common errors.\nfn get_error_guidance(error_message: &str) -> Option<String> {\n    let error_lower = error_message.to_lowercase();\n\n    // Config not found errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"config\") && (error_lower.contains(\"not found\") || error_lower.contains(\"missing\")) {\n        return Some(\"Run 'forge init' to create a configuration file, or check that ~/.forge/config.yaml exists.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // API/authentication errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"api key\") || error_lower.contains(\"authentication\") || error_lower.contains(\"unauthorized\") {\n        return Some(\"Check your API key in ~/.forge/config.yaml. Ensure the key is valid and not expired.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Rate limit errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"rate limit\") || error_lower.contains(\"too many requests\") {\n        return Some(\"Wait a moment before sending more commands. Rate limits reset automatically.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Network unreachable (no internet)\n    if error_lower.contains(\"network unreachable\") || error_lower.contains(\"no route to host\") {\n        return Some(\"⚠️  Network unreachable. Check your internet connection and try again when online. Last known state is cached.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // DNS resolution failures\n    if error_lower.contains(\"dns\") || error_lower.contains(\"failed to lookup address\") || error_lower.contains(\"name or service not known\") {\n        return Some(\"⚠️  DNS resolution failed. Check DNS settings or try using 8.8.8.8 as your DNS server. Click 'r' to retry.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Connection timeout\n    if error_lower.contains(\"timeout\") {\n        return Some(\"⏱️  Request timed out. The server may be slow or unreachable. Click 'r' to retry or check status.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Generic connection/network errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"connection\") || error_lower.contains(\"network\") {\n        return Some(\"⚠️  Connection failed. Check your internet connection. The API server may be temporarily unavailable. Click 'r' to retry.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Provider errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"provider\") && error_lower.contains(\"not found\") {\n        return Some(\"Check that the provider is installed and available in your PATH. Run 'forge --help' for setup instructions.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Tool execution errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"tool\") && error_lower.contains(\"failed\") {\n        return Some(\"The requested operation could not be completed. Check the tool output above for details.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Worker errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"worker\") && (error_lower.contains(\"spawn\") || error_lower.contains(\"launch\")) {\n        return Some(\"Ensure you have sufficient system resources and the worker binary is available.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Context gathering errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"context\") && error_lower.contains(\"failed\") {\n        return Some(\"The dashboard context could not be loaded. Try restarting the application.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Permission/access errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"permission\") || error_lower.contains(\"access denied\") || error_lower.contains(\"forbidden\") {\n        return Some(\"You don't have permission for this action. Check your configuration and credentials.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Resource not found errors (general)\n    if error_lower.contains(\"not found\") || error_lower.contains(\"does not exist\") {\n        return Some(\"The requested resource was not found. Verify the name and try again.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // JSON parsing errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"json\") || error_lower.contains(\"parse\") || error_lower.contains(\"deserialize\") {\n        return Some(\"There was a problem processing the response. The server may have returned unexpected data.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // IO errors\n    if error_lower.contains(\"io error\") || error_lower.contains(\"file\") && error_lower.contains(\"error\") {\n        return Some(\"A file system error occurred. Check file permissions and available disk space.\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    None\n}\n\n/// Result of an update operation.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\npub enum UpdateResult {\n    /// Update completed successfully with version info\n    Success {\n        /// Previous version\n        old_version: String,\n        /// New version\n        new_version: String,\n    },\n    /// Update failed with an error message\n    Failed(String),\n    /// Already up to date\n    AlreadyUpToDate,\n}\n\n/// Progress information for update downloads.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default)]\npub struct UpdateProgress {\n    /// Current status message\n    pub status: String,\n    /// Download progress percentage (0-100)\n    pub percent: u8,\n    /// Whether the update is in progress\n    pub in_progress: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Default for App {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl App {\n    /// Create a new app instance with default state.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        use tracing::info;\n\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ App::new() started\");\n\n        let now = Instant::now();\n\n        // Time DataManager initialization\n        let dm_start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ Creating DataManager...\");\n        let data_manager = DataManager::new();\n        info!(\"⏱️ DataManager created in {:?}\", dm_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Time ThemeManager initialization\n        let theme_start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ Loading ThemeManager...\");\n        let theme_manager = ThemeManager::load_config();\n        info!(\"⏱️ ThemeManager loaded in {:?}\", theme_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Initialize worker launcher and runtime (needed before chat backend for worker spawning)\n        let worker_launcher = WorkerLauncher::new();\n        let worker_runtime = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n            .enable_all()\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"Failed to create worker runtime\");\n\n        // Time chat backend initialization\n        let chat_start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ Initializing chat backend...\");\n        let chat_backend = Self::init_chat_backend_with_worker_spawner(&worker_runtime, &worker_launcher).map(Arc::new);\n        info!(\"⏱️ Chat backend initialized in {:?}\", chat_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Initialize config watcher for hot-reload (skip in test mode to save inotify instances)\n        let config_start = Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ Initializing config watcher...\");\n        let (config_watcher, config_rx, forge_config) = if cfg!(test) {\n            info!(\"⏱️ Skipping config watcher in test mode\");\n            (None, None, ForgeConfig::default())\n        } else {\n            match ConfigWatcher::new() {\n                Some((watcher, rx)) => {\n                    let config = watcher.current_config().clone();\n                    info!(\"⏱️ Config watcher initialized in {:?}\", config_start.elapsed());\n                    (Some(watcher), Some(rx), config)\n                }\n                None => {\n                    info!(\"⏱️ Config watcher not initialized (config file not found)\");\n                    (None, None, ForgeConfig::default())\n                }\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Calculate initial data poll interval from config\n        let data_poll_interval = Duration::from_millis(\n            forge_config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms.min(DEFAULT_DATA_POLL_INTERVAL_MS)\n        );\n\n        // Configure alert notifier from config\n        let mut data_manager = data_manager;\n        data_manager.configure_notifier(\n            forge_config.notifications.bell_on_critical,\n            forge_config.notifications.bell_on_warning,\n            forge_config.notifications.bell_interval_secs,\n            forge_config.notifications.visual_flash_on_critical,\n        );\n\n        // Initialize history manager and load previous history\n        let history_manager = forge_chat::HistoryManager::new().ok();\n\n        // Initialize session manager for team collaboration\n        let session_manager = SessionManager::new();\n\n        info!(\"⏱️ App::new() completed in {:?}\", start.elapsed());\n\n        Self {\n            current_view: View::default(),\n            previous_view: None,\n            focus_panel: FocusPanel::default(),\n            input_handler: InputHandler::new(),\n            should_quit: false,\n            show_help: false,\n            chat_input: String::new(),\n            status_message: None,\n            scroll_offset: 0,\n            data_manager,\n            theme_manager,\n            dirty: true,\n            cached_size: None,\n            last_poll_time: now,\n            cached_timestamp: None,\n            last_timestamp_update: now,\n            cached_layout_mode: None,\n            last_terminal_width: 0,\n            update_available: false,\n            update_in_progress: false,\n            update_progress: UpdateProgress::default(),\n            update_result_rx: None,\n            update_ready_for_restart: false,\n            last_update_check: now,\n            chat_backend,\n            chat_response_tx: None,\n            chat_response_rx: None,\n            chat_pending: false,\n            chat_spinner_frame: 0,\n            streaming_response: String::new(),\n            streaming_position: 0,\n            streaming_active: false,\n            pending_complete_response: None,\n            streaming_chunk_rx: None,\n            real_streaming_active: false,\n            streaming_query: None,\n            chat_history: Vec::new(),\n            chat_scroll_offset: 0,\n            worker_launcher,\n            worker_runtime,\n            show_kill_dialog: false,\n            kill_dialog_workers: Vec::new(),\n            kill_dialog_selected: 0,\n            kill_dialog_error: None,\n            priority_filter: None,\n            task_search_query: String::new(),\n            task_search_mode: false,\n            pending_chat_exchange: None,\n            show_task_detail: false,\n            selected_task_index: 0,\n            selected_alert_index: 0,\n            show_alert_detail: false,\n            _config_watcher: config_watcher,\n            config_rx,\n            forge_config,\n            data_poll_interval,\n            show_confirmation: false,\n            pending_action: None,\n            paused_workers: std::collections::HashSet::new(),\n            selected_worker_index: 0,\n            error_recovery: SharedErrorRecoveryManager::new(),\n            last_failed_query: None,\n            retry_available: false,\n            network_available: true,\n            network_error_message: None,\n            network_unavailable_since: None,\n            history_manager,\n            show_config_menu: false,\n            config_menu_type: None,\n            config_menu_selected: 0,\n            config_menu_editing: false,\n            config_menu_input: String::new(),\n            sessions_panel: crate::sessions_panel::SessionsPanel::new(),\n            server_client_tx: None,\n            server_client_rx: None,\n            user_session: None,\n            session_manager,\n            is_server_connected: false,\n            server_url: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new app with a custom status directory (for testing).\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pub fn with_status_dir(status_dir: std::path::PathBuf) -> Self {\n        let now = Instant::now();\n        Self {\n            current_view: View::default(),\n            previous_view: None,\n            focus_panel: FocusPanel::default(),\n            input_handler: InputHandler::new(),\n            should_quit: false,\n            show_help: false,\n            chat_input: String::new(),\n            status_message: None,\n            scroll_offset: 0,\n            data_manager: DataManager::with_status_dir(status_dir),\n            theme_manager: ThemeManager::new(),\n            dirty: true,\n            cached_size: None,\n            last_poll_time: now,\n            cached_timestamp: None,\n            last_timestamp_update: now,\n            cached_layout_mode: None,\n            last_terminal_width: 0,\n            update_available: false,\n            update_in_progress: false,\n            update_progress: UpdateProgress::default(),\n            update_result_rx: None,\n            update_ready_for_restart: false,\n            last_update_check: now,\n            chat_backend: None, // Don't initialize in test mode\n            chat_response_tx: None,\n            chat_response_rx: None,\n            chat_pending: false,\n            streaming_response: String::new(),\n            streaming_position: 0,\n            streaming_active: false,\n            pending_complete_response: None,\n            streaming_chunk_rx: None,\n            real_streaming_active: false,\n            streaming_query: None,\n            chat_history: Vec::new(),\n            chat_scroll_offset: 0,\n            worker_launcher: WorkerLauncher::new(),\n            worker_runtime: tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n                .enable_all()\n                .build()\n                .expect(\"Failed to create worker runtime\"),\n            show_kill_dialog: false,\n            kill_dialog_workers: Vec::new(),\n            kill_dialog_selected: 0,\n            kill_dialog_error: None,\n            priority_filter: None,\n            pending_chat_exchange: None,\n            show_task_detail: false,\n            selected_task_index: 0,\n            selected_alert_index: 0,\n            show_alert_detail: false,\n            _config_watcher: None, // Don't initialize in test mode\n            config_rx: None,\n            forge_config: ForgeConfig::default(),\n            data_poll_interval: Duration::from_millis(DEFAULT_DATA_POLL_INTERVAL_MS),\n            chat_spinner_frame: 0,\n            show_confirmation: false,\n            pending_action: None,\n            task_search_query: String::new(),\n            task_search_mode: false,\n            paused_workers: std::collections::HashSet::new(),\n            selected_worker_index: 0,\n            error_recovery: SharedErrorRecoveryManager::new(),\n            last_failed_query: None,\n            retry_available: false,\n            network_available: true,\n            network_error_message: None,\n            network_unavailable_since: None,\n            history_manager: None, // Don't initialize in test mode\n            show_config_menu: false,\n            config_menu_type: None,\n            config_menu_selected: 0,\n            config_menu_editing: false,\n            config_menu_input: String::new(),\n            sessions_panel: crate::sessions_panel::SessionsPanel::new(),\n            server_client_tx: None,\n            server_client_rx: None,\n            user_session: None,\n            session_manager: SessionManager::new(),\n            is_server_connected: false,\n            server_url: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Initialize chat backend from config.yaml with worker spawner support.\n    ///\n    /// Returns None if config is missing or initialization fails.\n    /// Errors are logged but don't prevent app startup.\n    fn init_chat_backend_with_worker_spawner(\n        _worker_runtime: &tokio::runtime::Runtime,\n        _worker_launcher: &WorkerLauncher,\n    ) -> Option<ChatBackend> {\n        use forge_chat::config::{AuditConfig, ConfirmationConfig, RateLimitConfig};\n        use forge_chat::spawner::RealWorkerSpawner;\n        use forge_chat::ChatProviderFactory;\n        use tracing::{error, info, warn};\n\n        let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        info!(\"⏱️ init_chat_backend_with_worker_spawner() started\");\n\n        // Load config from ~/.forge/config.yaml\n        let config_path = match dirs::home_dir() {\n            Some(home) => home.join(\".forge/config.yaml\"),\n            None => {\n                warn!(\"⏱️ Could not determine home directory (took {:?})\", start.elapsed());\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n\n        info!(\n            \"⏱️ Initializing chat backend from {}\",\n            config_path.display()\n        );\n\n        if !config_path.exists() {\n            warn!(\n                \"⏱️ Chat config not found at {} (took {:?})\",\n                config_path.display(),\n                start.elapsed()\n            );\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let read_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        let config_str = match std::fs::read_to_string(&config_path) {\n            Ok(s) => s,\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\n                    \"⏱️ Failed to read chat config: {} (took {:?})\",\n                    e,\n                    start.elapsed()\n                );\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n        info!(\"⏱️ Config file read in {:?}\", read_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Parse the full config YAML\n        let parse_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        let yaml: serde_yaml::Value = match serde_yaml::from_str(&config_str) {\n            Ok(v) => v,\n            Err(e) => {\n                warn!(\n                    \"⏱️ Failed to parse config YAML: {} (took {:?})\",\n                    e,\n                    start.elapsed()\n                );\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n        info!(\"⏱️ YAML parsed in {:?}\", parse_start.elapsed());\n\n        // Extract chat_backend section\n        let chat_backend = yaml.get(\"chat_backend\")?;\n        let command = chat_backend.get(\"command\")?.as_str()?;\n\n        // Read optional args (not required — providers may handle args internally)\n        let args: Vec<String> = chat_backend\n            .get(\"args\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_sequence())\n            .map(|seq| {\n                seq.iter()\n                    .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n                    .collect()\n            })\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        // Read model (optional, defaults to \"sonnet\")\n        let model = chat_backend\n            .get(\"model\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or(\"sonnet\")\n            .to_string();\n\n        // Determine provider: explicit `provider` field takes precedence; fall back to\n        // inferring from `command` for backwards compatibility (opencode → opencode,\n        // anything else → claude-cli).\n        let provider_name = chat_backend\n            .get(\"provider\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| {\n                if command == \"opencode\" {\n                    \"opencode\"\n                } else {\n                    \"claude-cli\"\n                }\n            });\n\n        info!(\"⏱️ Using provider: {}\", provider_name);\n\n        // Warn about command/provider field mismatches before delegating to factory.\n        match provider_name {\n            \"opencode\" if command != \"opencode\" => {\n                warn!(\n                    \"Provider is 'opencode' but command is '{}' — using command as binary path\",\n                    command\n                );\n            }\n            \"claude-cli\" if command == \"opencode\" => {\n                warn!(\n                    \"Provider is 'claude-cli' but command is 'opencode' — this may not work as \\\n                     expected. Set provider: opencode to use the OpenCode provider.\"\n                );\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n\n        // Read model_aliases (only consumed by the opencode provider).\n        let model_aliases = chat_backend\n            .get(\"model_aliases\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_mapping())\n            .map(|m| {\n                m.iter()\n                    .filter_map(|(k, v)| {\n                        let key = k.as_str()?.to_string();\n                        let val = v.as_str()?.to_string();\n                        Some((key, val))\n                    })\n                    .collect::<std::collections::HashMap<String, String>>()\n            });\n\n        // Use the factory to build the ProviderConfig from the provider name string.\n        let provider_config = match ChatProviderFactory::create_config(\n            provider_name,\n            command,\n            &model,\n            args,\n            model_aliases,\n        ) {\n            Ok(config) => config,\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\"Failed to create provider config: {}\", e);\n                return None;\n            }\n        };\n\n        let chat_config = ChatConfig {\n            provider: provider_config,\n            rate_limit: RateLimitConfig::default(),\n            audit: AuditConfig::default(),\n            confirmations: ConfirmationConfig::default(),\n        };\n\n        // Get workspace path\n        let home = std::env::var(\"HOME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"/home/coder\".to_string());\n        let workspace = std::env::var(\"FORGE_WORKSPACE\")\n            .map(std::path::PathBuf::from)\n            .unwrap_or_else(|_| std::path::PathBuf::from(&home).join(\"forge\"));\n\n        // Create RealWorkerSpawner with default launcher detection\n        let worker_spawner: Arc<dyn forge_chat::spawner::WorkerSpawner> = match RealWorkerSpawner::with_default_launcher(workspace) {\n            Some(spawner) => Arc::new(spawner),\n            None => {\n                warn!(\"⏱️ No worker launcher script found, worker spawning via chat will not be available\");\n                Arc::new(forge_chat::spawner::NoOpWorkerSpawner)\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Initialize backend (async, but we block here during startup)\n        let runtime_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n        match tokio::runtime::Runtime::new() {\n            Ok(rt) => {\n                info!(\n                    \"⏱️ Created tokio runtime for chat backend in {:?}\",\n                    runtime_start.elapsed()\n                );\n                let backend_start = std::time::Instant::now();\n                match rt.block_on(ChatBackend::new(chat_config)) {\n                    Ok(backend) => {\n                        let backend = backend.with_worker_spawner(worker_spawner);\n                        info!(\n                            \"⏱️ ✅ Chat backend initialized successfully in {:?} (total: {:?})\",\n                            backend_start.elapsed(),\n                            start.elapsed()\n                        );\n                        Some(backend)\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        error!(\n                            \"⏱️ ❌ Failed to initialize chat backend: {} (took {:?})\",\n                            e,\n                            start.elapsed()\n                        );\n                        None\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\n                    \"⏱️ ❌ Failed to create tokio runtime: {} (took {:?})\",\n                    e,\n                    start.elapsed()\n                );\n                None\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the current view.\n    pub fn current_view(&self) -> View {\n        self.current_view\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the current focus panel.\n    pub fn focus_panel(&self) -> FocusPanel {\n        self.focus_panel\n    }\n\n    /// Returns whether the app should quit.\n    pub fn should_quit(&self) -> bool {\n        self.should_quit\n    }\n\n    /// Returns whether help overlay is visible.\n    pub fn show_help(&self) -> bool {\n        self.show_help\n    }\n\n    /// Returns whether kill dialog is visible.\n    pub fn show_kill_dialog(&self) -> bool {\n        self.show_kill_dialog\n    }\n\n    /// Mark the UI as dirty (needs redraw).\n    fn mark_dirty(&mut self) {\n        self.dirty = true;\n    }\n\n    /// Handle local chat commands (commands processed by TUI, not sent to backend).\n    ///\n    /// Returns true if the command was handled locally, false if it should be sent to backend.\n    fn handle_chat_command(&mut self, query: &str) -> bool {\n        let trimmed = query.trim();\n\n        // Check for slash commands\n        if !trimmed.starts_with('/') {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Parse command and arguments\n        let parts: Vec<&str> = trimmed[1..].splitn(2, ' ').collect();\n        let command = parts[0].to_lowercase();\n        let args = parts.get(1).map(|s| s.trim());\n\n        match command.as_str() {\n            \"save\" => {\n                self.save_chat_history();\n                true\n            }\n            \"load\" => {\n                self.load_chat_history();\n                true\n            }\n            \"export\" => {\n                let path = args.map(std::path::PathBuf::from);\n                self.export_chat_history(path);\n                true\n            }\n            \"clear\" => {\n                self.chat_history.clear();\n                self.chat_scroll_offset = 0;\n                self.status_message = Some(\"Chat history cleared\".to_string());\n                info!(\"Chat history cleared by user command\");\n                true\n            }\n            \"history\" => {\n                // Show history info\n                let count = self.chat_history.len();\n                let msg = if count == 0 {\n                    \"No chat history. Use /load to restore previous sessions.\".to_string()\n                } else {\n                    format!(\"Chat history: {} exchanges in current session\", count)\n                };\n                self.status_message = Some(msg);\n                true\n            }\n            \"stats\" => {\n                self.show_history_stats();\n                true\n            }\n            \"help\" => {\n                // Show available commands\n                let help_text = \"Commands: /save /load /export [path] /clear /history /stats /help\";\n                self.chat_history.push(ChatExchange {\n                    user_query: \"/help\".to_string(),\n                    assistant_response: help_text.to_string(),\n                    timestamp: chrono::Local::now().format(\"%H:%M:%S\").to_string(),\n                    is_error: false,\n                    tool_calls: vec![],\n                    side_effects: vec![],\n                    confirmation: None,\n                    metadata: ResponseMetadata::default(),\n                    error_guidance: None,\n                });\n                // Limit history to 10 exchanges\n                if self.chat_history.len() > 10 {\n                    self.chat_history.remove(0);\n                }\n                true\n            }\n            _ => {\n                // Unknown slash command - let it go to the backend\n                false\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Save current chat history to disk.\n    fn save_chat_history(&mut self) {\n        let history_manager = match &self.history_manager {\n            Some(hm) => hm,\n            None => {\n                self.status_message = Some(\"History manager not initialized\".to_string());\n                return;\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Convert ChatExchange to HistoryEntry\n        let entries: Vec<forge_chat::HistoryEntry> = self\n            .chat_history\n            .iter()\n            .map(|exchange| forge_chat::HistoryEntry {\n                timestamp: exchange.timestamp.clone(),\n                user_query: exchange.user_query.clone(),\n                assistant_response: exchange.assistant_response.clone(),\n                is_error: exchange.is_error,\n                metadata: Some(forge_chat::HistoryMetadata {\n                    duration_ms: exchange.metadata.duration_ms,\n                    cost_usd: exchange.metadata.cost_usd,\n                    provider: exchange.metadata.provider.clone(),\n                }),\n                tool_calls: exchange\n                    .tool_calls\n                    .iter()\n                    .map(|tc| forge_chat::HistoryToolCall {\n                        name: tc.name.clone(),\n                        success: tc.success,\n                        message: Some(tc.message.clone()),\n                    })\n                    .collect(),\n                session_id: None,\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        // Save in background (avoid blocking UI)\n        let history_path = history_manager.history_path().clone();\n        let session_id = history_manager.session_id().to_string();\n        std::thread::spawn(move || {\n            use std::io::Write;\n            match std::fs::OpenOptions::new()\n                .create(true)\n
