{}\", worker_id_clone, e);\n                self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Failed to spawn {} worker: {}\", model_name, e));\n            }\n        }\n\n        self.mark_dirty();\n    }\n\n    /// Discover workers and show the kill dialog.\n    fn discover_workers_for_kill_dialog(&mut self) {\n        use forge_worker::discovery::discover_workers;\n        use tracing::{error, info};\n\n        info!(\"Discovering workers for kill dialog\");\n\n        // Discover workers using the runtime\n        let result = self\n            .worker_runtime\n            .block_on(async { discover_workers().await });\n\n        match result {\n            Ok(discovery) => {\n                let worker_count = discovery.workers.len();\n                info!(\"Discovered {} workers for kill dialog\", worker_count);\n\n                if worker_count == 0 {\n                    self.kill_dialog_error = Some(\"No active workers found\".to_string());\n                    self.kill_dialog_workers = Vec::new();\n                } else {\n                    self.kill_dialog_error = None;\n                    self.kill_dialog_workers = discovery.workers;\n                }\n\n                self.kill_dialog_selected = 0;\n                self.show_kill_dialog = true;\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\"Failed to discover workers: {}\", e);\n                self.kill_dialog_error = Some(format!(\"Failed to discover workers: {}\", e));\n                self.kill_dialog_workers = Vec::new();\n                self.kill_dialog_selected = 0;\n                self.show_kill_dialog = true;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Kill the currently selected worker in the kill dialog.\n    fn kill_selected_worker(&mut self) {\n        use forge_worker::tmux::{kill_session, session_exists};\n        use tracing::{error, info, warn};\n\n        if self.kill_dialog_selected >= self.kill_dialog_workers.len() {\n            self.kill_dialog_error = Some(\"No worker selected\".to_string());\n            self.mark_dirty();\n            return;\n        }\n\n        let worker = &self.kill_dialog_workers[self.kill_dialog_selected].clone();\n        let session_name = worker.session_name.clone();\n\n        info!(\n            \"Killing worker: {} (session: {})\",\n            worker.suffix, session_name\n        );\n\n        // First check if session exists (to handle already-dead workers gracefully)\n        let exists = self\n            .worker_runtime\n            .block_on(async { session_exists(&session_name).await });\n\n        match exists {\n            Ok(true) => {\n                // Session exists, proceed to kill\n                let result = self\n                    .worker_runtime\n                    .block_on(async { kill_session(&session_name).await });\n\n                match result {\n                    Ok(()) => {\n                        info!(\"Successfully killed worker session: {}\", session_name);\n                        self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Killed worker: {}\", worker.suffix));\n\n                        // Remove the killed worker from the list\n                        self.kill_dialog_workers.remove(self.kill_dialog_selected);\n\n                        // Adjust selection if needed\n                        if self.kill_dialog_selected >= self.kill_dialog_workers.len()\n                            && self.kill_dialog_selected > 0\n                        {\n                            self.kill_dialog_selected -= 1;\n                        }\n\n                        // Close dialog if no workers left\n                        if self.kill_dialog_workers.is_empty() {\n                            self.show_kill_dialog = false;\n                            self.kill_dialog_error = None;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        error!(\"Failed to kill worker {}: {}\", session_name, e);\n                        self.kill_dialog_error = Some(format!(\"Failed to kill worker: {}\", e));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            Ok(false) => {\n                // Session doesn't exist - worker already terminated\n                warn!(\"Worker session already terminated: {}\", session_name);\n                self.kill_dialog_error = Some(format!(\n                    \"Worker '{}' already terminated or session not found\",\n                    worker.suffix\n                ));\n\n                // Remove from list since it's already dead\n                self.kill_dialog_workers.remove(self.kill_dialog_selected);\n                if self.kill_dialog_selected >= self.kill_dialog_workers.len()\n                    && self.kill_dialog_selected > 0\n                {\n                    self.kill_dialog_selected -= 1;\n                }\n                if self.kill_dialog_workers.is_empty() {\n                    self.show_kill_dialog = false;\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(\"Failed to check session existence: {}\", e);\n                // Try to kill anyway - the kill might still work\n                let result = self\n                    .worker_runtime\n                    .block_on(async { kill_session(&session_name).await });\n                match result {\n                    Ok(()) => {\n                        info!(\"Successfully killed worker session: {}\", session_name);\n                        self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Killed worker: {}\", worker.suffix));\n                        self.kill_dialog_workers.remove(self.kill_dialog_selected);\n                        if self.kill_dialog_selected >= self.kill_dialog_workers.len()\n                            && self.kill_dialog_selected > 0\n                        {\n                            self.kill_dialog_selected -= 1;\n                        }\n                        if self.kill_dialog_workers.is_empty() {\n                            self.show_kill_dialog = false;\n                            self.kill_dialog_error = None;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        self.kill_dialog_error = Some(format!(\"Failed to kill worker: {}\", e));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        self.mark_dirty();\n    }\n\n    /// Show confirmation dialog for pausing the selected worker.\n    fn show_pause_worker_confirmation(&mut self) {\n        // Get sorted list of worker IDs\n        let mut workers: Vec<_> = self.data_manager.worker_data.workers.keys().collect();\n        workers.sort();\n\n        if workers.is_empty() {\n            self.status_message = Some(\"No workers to pause\".to_string());\n            self.mark_dirty();\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Ensure selection is within bounds\n        if self.selected_worker_index >= workers.len() {\n            self.selected_worker_index = 0;\n        }\n\n        let worker_id = workers[self.selected_worker_index].clone();\n\n        // Check if already paused\n        if self.paused_workers.contains(&worker_id) {\n            self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Worker {} is already paused\", worker_id));\n            self.mark_dirty();\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Show confirmation dialog\n        self.pending_action = Some(PendingAction::PauseWorker { worker_id });\n        self.show_confirmation = true;\n        self.mark_dirty();\n    }\n\n    /// Show confirmation dialog for pausing all workers.\n    fn show_pause_all_workers_confirmation(&mut self) {\n        let workers = &self.data_manager.worker_data.workers;\n\n        if workers.is_empty() {\n            self.status_message = Some(\"No workers to pause\".to_string());\n            self.mark_dirty();\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Count workers that are not already paused\n        let count = workers\n            .keys()\n            .filter(|id| !self.paused_workers.contains(*id))\n            .count();\n\n        if count == 0 {\n            self.status_message = Some(\"All workers are already paused\".to_string());\n            self.mark_dirty();\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Show confirmation dialog\n        self.pending_action = Some(PendingAction::PauseAllWorkers { count });\n        self.show_confirmation = true;\n        self.mark_dirty();\n    }\n\n    /// Show confirmation dialog for resuming the selected worker.\n    fn show_resume_worker_confirmation(&mut self) {\n        // Get sorted list of worker IDs\n        let mut workers: Vec<_> = self.data_manager.worker_data.workers.keys().collect();\n        workers.sort();\n\n        if workers.is_empty() {\n            self.status_message = Some(\"No workers to resume\".to_string());\n            self.mark_dirty();\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Ensure selection is within bounds\n        if self.selected_worker_index >= workers.len() {\n            self.selected_worker_index = 0;\n        }\n\n        let worker_id = workers[self.selected_worker_index].clone();\n\n        // Check if actually paused\n        if !self.paused_workers.contains(&worker_id) {\n            self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Worker {} is not paused\", worker_id));\n            self.mark_dirty();\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Show confirmation dialog\n        self.pending_action = Some(PendingAction::ResumeWorker { worker_id });\n        self.show_confirmation = true;\n        self.mark_dirty();\n    }\n\n    /// Show confirmation dialog for resuming all workers.\n    fn show_resume_all_workers_confirmation(&mut self) {\n        if self.paused_workers.is_empty() {\n            self.status_message = Some(\"No paused workers to resume\".to_string());\n            self.mark_dirty();\n            return;\n        }\n\n        let count = self.paused_workers.len();\n\n        // Show confirmation dialog\n        self.pending_action = Some(PendingAction::ResumeAllWorkers { count });\n        self.show_confirmation = true;\n        self.mark_dirty();\n    }\n\n    /// Pause the currently selected worker in the workers list.\n    fn pause_selected_worker(&mut self) {\n        use forge_worker::tmux;\n\n        // Get sorted list of worker IDs\n        let mut workers: Vec<_> = self.data_manager.worker_data.workers.keys().collect();\n        workers.sort();\n\n        if workers.is_empty() {\n            self.status_message = Some(\"No workers to pause\".to_string());\n            self.mark_dirty();\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Ensure selection is within bounds\n        if self.selected_worker_index >= workers.len() {\n            self.selected_worker_index = 0;\n        }\n\n        let worker_id = workers[self.selected_worker_index].clone();\n\n        // Check if already paused\n        if self.paused_workers.contains(&worker_id) {\n            self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Worker {} is already paused\", worker_id));\n            self.mark_dirty();\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Send pause signal to tmux session\n        let worker_id_clone = worker_id.clone();\n        let result = self\n            .worker_runtime\n            .block_on(async { tmux::pause_session(&worker_id_clone).await });\n\n        match result {\n            Ok(()) => {\n                // Add to paused set\n                self.paused_workers.insert(worker_id.clone());\n                self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Paused worker: {}\", worker_id));\n                info!(\"Paused worker: {}\", worker_id);\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Failed to pause worker: {}\", e));\n                warn!(\"Failed to pause worker {}: {}\", worker_id, e);\n            }\n        }\n\n        self.mark_dirty();\n    }\n\n    /// Pause all workers.\n    fn pause_all_workers(&mut self) {\n        use forge_worker::tmux;\n\n        let workers = &self.data_manager.worker_data.workers;\n\n        if workers.is_empty() {\n            self.status_message = Some(\"No workers to pause\".to_string());\n            self.mark_dirty();\n            return;\n        }\n\n        let mut paused_count = 0;\n        let mut failed_count = 0;\n\n        for worker_id in workers.keys() {\n            if !self.paused_workers.contains(worker_id) {\n                let worker_id_clone = worker_id.clone();\n                let result = self\n                    .worker_runtime\n                    .block_on(async { tmux::pause_session(&worker_id_clone).await });\n\n                match result {\n                    Ok(()) => {\n                        self.paused_workers.insert(worker_id.clone());\n                        paused_count += 1;\n                    }\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        warn!(\"Failed to pause worker {}: {}\", worker_id, e);\n                        failed_count += 1;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        if failed_count > 0 {\n            self.status_message = Some(format!(\n                \"Paused {} workers ({} failed)\",\n                paused_count, failed_count\n            ));\n        } else {\n            self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Paused {} workers\", paused_count));\n        }\n\n        info!(\"Paused {} workers\", paused_count);\n        self.mark_dirty();\n    }\n\n    /// Resume the currently selected worker in the workers list.\n    fn resume_selected_worker(&mut self) {\n        use forge_worker::tmux;\n\n        // Get sorted list of worker IDs\n        let mut workers: Vec<_> = self.data_manager.worker_data.workers.keys().collect();\n        workers.sort();\n\n        if workers.is_empty() {\n            self.status_message = Some(\"No workers to resume\".to_string());\n            self.mark_dirty();\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Ensure selection is within bounds\n        if self.selected_worker_index >= workers.len() {\n            self.selected_worker_index = 0;\n        }\n\n        let worker_id = workers[self.selected_worker_index].clone();\n\n        // Check if actually paused\n        if !self.paused_workers.contains(&worker_id) {\n            self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Worker {} is not paused\", worker_id));\n            self.mark_dirty();\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Send resume signal to tmux session\n        let worker_id_clone = worker_id.clone();\n        let result = self\n            .worker_runtime\n            .block_on(async { tmux::resume_session(&worker_id_clone).await });\n\n        match result {\n            Ok(()) => {\n                // Remove from paused set\n                self.paused_workers.remove(&worker_id);\n                self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Resumed worker: {}\", worker_id));\n                info!(\"Resumed worker: {}\", worker_id);\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Failed to resume worker: {}\", e));\n                warn!(\"Failed to resume worker {}: {}\", worker_id, e);\n            }\n        }\n\n        self.mark_dirty();\n    }\n\n    /// Resume all paused workers.\n    fn resume_all_workers(&mut self) {\n        use forge_worker::tmux;\n\n        if self.paused_workers.is_empty() {\n            self.status_message = Some(\"No paused workers to resume\".to_string());\n            self.mark_dirty();\n            return;\n        }\n\n        let resumed_count = self.paused_workers.len();\n        let mut failed_count = 0;\n\n        // Collect worker IDs to avoid modifying set while iterating\n        let worker_ids: Vec<_> = self.paused_workers.iter().cloned().collect();\n\n        for worker_id in worker_ids {\n            let result = self\n                .worker_runtime\n                .block_on(async { tmux::resume_session(&worker_id).await });\n\n            match result {\n                Ok(()) => {\n                    self.paused_workers.remove(&worker_id);\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    warn!(\"Failed to resume worker {}: {}\", worker_id, e);\n                    failed_count += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        let actually_resumed = resumed_count - failed_count;\n        if failed_count > 0 {\n            self.status_message = Some(format!(\n                \"Resumed {} workers ({} failed)\",\n                actually_resumed, failed_count\n            ));\n        } else {\n            self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Resumed {} workers\", actually_resumed));\n        }\n\n        info!(\"Resumed {} workers\", actually_resumed);\n        self.mark_dirty();\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a worker is paused.\n    pub fn is_worker_paused(&self, worker_id: &str) -> bool {\n        self.paused_workers.contains(worker_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Acknowledge the currently selected alert in the alerts list.\n    fn acknowledge_selected_alert(&mut self) {\n        let alerts = self.data_manager.alert_manager.alerts_by_severity();\n        if let Some(alert) = alerts.get(self.selected_alert_index) {\n            let alert_id = alert.id;\n            let worker_id = alert.worker_id.clone();\n            self.data_manager.alert_manager.acknowledge(alert_id);\n            self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Acknowledged alert for {}\", worker_id));\n            self.mark_dirty();\n        } else {\n            self.status_message = Some(\"No alert selected\".to_string());\n            self.mark_dirty();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Acknowledge all alerts.\n    fn acknowledge_all_alerts(&mut self) {\n        let count = self.data_manager.acknowledge_all_alerts();\n        if count > 0 {\n            self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Acknowledged {} alert(s)\", count));\n        } else {\n            self.status_message = Some(\"No alerts to acknowledge\".to_string());\n        }\n        self.mark_dirty();\n    }\n\n    /// Handle kill dialog key navigation.\n    fn handle_kill_dialog_key(&mut self, key: KeyEvent) {\n        use crossterm::event::KeyCode;\n\n        match key.code {\n            KeyCode::Char('k') | KeyCode::Up\n                // Move selection up\n                if self.kill_dialog_selected > 0 => {\n                    self.kill_dialog_selected -= 1;\n                    self.kill_dialog_error = None; // Clear error on navigation\n                    self.mark_dirty();\n                }\n            KeyCode::Char('j') | KeyCode::Down\n                // Move selection down\n                if self.kill_dialog_selected + 1 < self.kill_dialog_workers.len() => {\n                    self.kill_dialog_selected += 1;\n                    self.kill_dialog_error = None; // Clear error on navigation\n                    self.mark_dirty();\n                }\n            KeyCode::Enter\n                // Request confirmation before killing the selected worker\n                if self.kill_dialog_selected < self.kill_dialog_workers.len() => {\n                    let worker = &self.kill_dialog_workers[self.kill_dialog_selected];\n                    let suffix = worker.suffix.clone();\n                    let worker_type = worker.worker_type.to_string();\n\n                    self.pending_action = Some(PendingAction::KillWorker {\n                        suffix,\n                        worker_type\n                    });\n                    self.show_confirmation = true;\n                    self.mark_dirty();\n                }\n            KeyCode::Esc | KeyCode::Char('q') => {\n                // Close dialog\n                self.show_kill_dialog = false;\n                self.kill_dialog_error = None;\n                self.mark_dirty();\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Handle confirmation dialog key events.\n    fn handle_confirmation_dialog_key(&mut self, key: KeyEvent) {\n        use crossterm::event::KeyCode;\n\n        match key.code {\n            KeyCode::Enter | KeyCode::Char('y') | KeyCode::Char('Y') => {\n                // Confirm the action\n                self.show_confirmation = false;\n                if let Some(action) = self.pending_action.take() {\n                    self.execute_pending_action(action);\n                }\n                self.mark_dirty();\n            }\n            KeyCode::Esc | KeyCode::Char('n') | KeyCode::Char('N') | KeyCode::Char('q') => {\n                // Cancel the action\n                self.show_confirmation = false;\n                self.pending_action = None;\n                self.status_message = Some(\"Action cancelled\".to_string());\n                self.mark_dirty();\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Execute a pending action after confirmation.\n    fn execute_pending_action(&mut self, action: PendingAction) {\n        match action {\n            PendingAction::SpawnWorker(executor) => {\n                // Execute spawn logic\n                self.spawn_worker(executor);\n            }\n            PendingAction::KillWorker { suffix: _, worker_type: _ } => {\n                // Execute kill logic - called from kill dialog after confirmation\n                self.kill_selected_worker();\n            }\n            PendingAction::PauseWorker { .. } => {\n                self.pause_selected_worker();\n            }\n            PendingAction::PauseAllWorkers { .. } => {\n                self.pause_all_workers();\n            }\n            PendingAction::ResumeWorker { .. } => {\n                self.resume_selected_worker();\n            }\n            PendingAction::ResumeAllWorkers { .. } => {\n                self.resume_all_workers();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Handle task detail overlay key navigation.\n    fn handle_task_detail_key(&mut self, key: KeyEvent) {\n        use crossterm::event::KeyCode;\n\n        match key.code {\n            KeyCode::Esc | KeyCode::Char('q') => {\n                // Close detail overlay\n                self.show_task_detail = false;\n                self.mark_dirty();\n            }\n            KeyCode::Char('k') | KeyCode::Up\n                // Move selection up in task list\n                if self.selected_task_index > 0 => {\n                    self.selected_task_index -= 1;\n                    self.mark_dirty();\n                }\n            KeyCode::Char('j') | KeyCode::Down => {\n                // Move selection down in task list\n                self.selected_task_index += 1;\n                self.mark_dirty();\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Handle alert detail overlay key navigation.\n    fn handle_alert_detail_key(&mut self, key: KeyEvent) {\n        use crossterm::event::KeyCode;\n\n        // Get alert count for bounds checking\n        let alerts = self.data_manager.alert_manager.alerts_by_severity();\n        let alert_count = alerts.len();\n\n        match key.code {\n            KeyCode::Esc | KeyCode::Char('q') => {\n                // Close detail overlay\n                self.show_alert_detail = false;\n                self.mark_dirty();\n            }\n            KeyCode::Char('k') | KeyCode::Up => {\n                // Move selection up in alert list\n                if self.selected_alert_index > 0 {\n                    self.selected_alert_index -= 1;\n                    self.mark_dirty();\n                }\n            }\n            KeyCode::Char('j') | KeyCode::Down => {\n                // Move selection down in alert list\n                if self.selected_alert_index < alert_count.saturating_sub(1) {\n                    self.selected_alert_index += 1;\n                    self.mark_dirty();\n                }\n            }\n            KeyCode::Char('a') => {\n                // Acknowledge the selected alert\n                if !alerts.is_empty() {\n                    let idx = self.selected_alert_index.min(alerts.len() - 1);\n                    let alert_id = alerts[idx].id;\n                    let alert_title = alerts[idx].title.clone();\n                    self.data_manager.alert_manager.acknowledge(alert_id);\n                    self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Alert acknowledged: {}\", alert_title));\n                    self.mark_dirty();\n                }\n            }\n            KeyCode::Enter => {\n                // Enter in overlay mode also acknowledges the alert\n                if !alerts.is_empty() {\n                    let idx = self.selected_alert_index.min(alerts.len() - 1);\n                    let alert_id = alerts[idx].id;\n                    let alert_title = alerts[idx].title.clone();\n                    self.data_manager.alert_manager.acknowledge(alert_id);\n                    self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Alert acknowledged: {}\", alert_title));\n                    self.mark_dirty();\n                }\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Handle config menu overlay key navigation.\n    fn handle_config_menu_key(&mut self, key: KeyEvent) {\n        use crossterm::event::KeyCode;\n\n        // Get current menu items for bounds checking and validation\n        let menu_type = match self.config_menu_type {\n            Some(t) => t,\n            None => return,\n        };\n\n        let items = match menu_type {\n            ConfigMenuType::Settings => build_settings_items(&self.forge_config),\n            ConfigMenuType::Budget => build_budget_items(&self.forge_config),\n            ConfigMenuType::Worker => build_worker_items(&self.forge_config),\n        };\n\n        let item_count = items.len();\n\n        match key.code {\n            KeyCode::Esc => {\n                // Close config menu (cancel any editing)\n                self.show_config_menu = false;\n                self.config_menu_editing = false;\n                self.config_menu_input.clear();\n                self.mark_dirty();\n            }\n            KeyCode::Char('\\n') | KeyCode::Enter => {\n                if self.config_menu_editing {\n                    // Validate and save the edited value\n                    if let Some(item) = items.get(self.config_menu_selected) {\n                        if item.editable {\n                            // Validate input\n                            match item.input_type.validate(&self.config_menu_input) {\n                                Ok(validated) => {\n                                    // Apply the change to config\n                                    if self.apply_menu_item_change(menu_type, item.label, &validated) {\n                                        // Save config to file\n                                        match self.forge_config.save() {\n                                            Ok(()) => {\n                                                self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Saved: {} = {}\", item.label, validated));\n                                                // Hot-reload will be triggered by ConfigWatcher\n                                            }\n                                            Err(e) => {\n                                                self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Error saving config: {}\", e));\n                                            }\n                                        }\n                                    }\n                                }\n                                Err(e) => {\n                                    self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Invalid value: {}\", e));\n                                    // Don't exit editing mode on validation error\n                                    self.mark_dirty();\n                                    return;\n                                }\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    self.config_menu_editing = false;\n                    self.config_menu_input.clear();\n                } else {\n                    // Enter editing mode for selected item\n                    if let Some(item) = items.get(self.config_menu_selected) {\n                        if item.editable {\n                            self.config_menu_editing = true;\n                            // Pre-populate input with current value (strip units for editing)\n                            self.config_menu_input = self.extract_edit_value(item);\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                self.mark_dirty();\n            }\n            KeyCode::Char(c) if !self.config_menu_editing => {\n                // Navigation in non-editing mode\n                match c {\n                    'j'\n                        // Down with bounds checking\n                        if self.config_menu_selected < item_count.saturating_sub(1) => {\n                            self.config_menu_selected += 1;\n                            self.mark_dirty();\n                        }\n                    'k'\n                        // Up with bounds checking\n                        if self.config_menu_selected > 0 => {\n                            self.config_menu_selected -= 1;\n                            self.mark_dirty();\n                        }\n                    'q' => {\n                        self.show_config_menu = false;\n                        self.config_menu_input.clear();\n                        self.mark_dirty();\n                    }\n                    _ => {}\n                }\n            }\n            KeyCode::Char(c) if self.config_menu_editing => {\n                // Add character to input buffer\n                self.config_menu_input.push(c);\n                self.mark_dirty();\n            }\n            KeyCode::Backspace if self.config_menu_editing => {\n                self.config_menu_input.pop();\n                self.mark_dirty();\n            }\n            KeyCode::Up if !self.config_menu_editing\n                && self.config_menu_selected > 0 => {\n                    self.config_menu_selected -= 1;\n                    self.mark_dirty();\n                }\n            KeyCode::Down if !self.config_menu_editing\n                && self.config_menu_selected < item_count.saturating_sub(1) => {\n                    self.config_menu_selected += 1;\n                    self.mark_dirty();\n                }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Extract an editable value from a config item (strips units/formatting).\n    fn extract_edit_value(&self, item: &crate::config_menu::ConfigMenuItem) -> String {\n        let value = &item.value;\n\n        // Handle values with units (e.g., \"1000 ms\", \"60%\", \"$100.00\", \"30 min\")\n        match item.input_type {\n            crate::config_menu::ConfigInputType::Integer => {\n                // Extract just the number\n                value.split_whitespace().next().unwrap_or(value).to_string()\n            }\n            crate::config_menu::ConfigInputType::Float => {\n                // Remove currency symbol and extract number\n                value.trim_start_matches('$').split_whitespace().next().unwrap_or(value).to_string()\n            }\n            crate::config_menu::ConfigInputType::Text => {\n                value.to_string()\n            }\n            crate::config_menu::ConfigInputType::Select { .. } => {\n                // For select, return the current value as-is\n                value.to_string()\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Apply a config change from a menu item to the appropriate field.\n    ///\n    /// Returns true if the change was applied, false if the field wasn't recognized.\n    fn apply_menu_item_change(&mut self, menu_type: ConfigMenuType, label: &str, value: &str) -> bool {\n        match menu_type {\n            ConfigMenuType::Settings => {\n                match label {\n                    \"Refresh Interval\" => {\n                        if let Ok(v) = value.parse::<u64>() {\n                            self.forge_config.dashboard.refresh_interval_ms = v;\n                            // Update the data poll interval immediately\n                            self.data_poll_interval = std::time::Duration::from_millis(v);\n                            return true;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    \"Max FPS\" => {\n                        if let Ok(v) = value.parse::<u64>() {\n                            self.forge_config.dashboard.max_fps = v;\n                            return true;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    \"Default Layout\" => {\n                        self.forge_config.dashboard.default_layout = value.to_lowercase();\n                        return true;\n                    }\n                    \"Theme\" => {\n                        self.forge_config.theme.name = Some(value.to_string());\n                        return true;\n                    }\n                    \"Log Retention\" => {\n                        let days = match value {\n                            \"forever\" => 0,\n                            _ => value.trim_end_matches(\" days\").parse::<u64>().unwrap_or(7),\n                        };\n                        self.forge_config.dashboard.log_retention_days = days;\n                        return true;\n                    }\n                    _ => {}\n                }\n            }\n            ConfigMenuType::Budget => {\n                match label {\n                    \"Cost Tracking\" => {\n                        self.forge_config.cost_tracking.enabled = value == \"enabled\";\n                        return true;\n                    }\n                    \"Monthly Budget\" => {\n                        // Parse currency value (e.g., \"100.00\" or \"$100.00\")\n                        let clean = value.trim_start_matches('$');\n                        if let Ok(v) = clean.parse::<f64>() {\n                            self.forge_config.cost_tracking.monthly_budget_usd = Some(v);\n                            return true;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    \"Warning Threshold\" => {\n                        // Parse percentage value\n                        if let Ok(v) = value.trim_end_matches('%').parse::<u8>() {\n                            self.forge_config.cost_tracking.budget_warning_threshold = v;\n                            return true;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    \"Critical Threshold\" => {\n                        if let Ok(v) = value.trim_end_matches('%').parse::<u8>() {\n                            self.forge_config.cost_tracking.budget_critical_threshold = v;\n                            return true;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    \"Sonnet Cost/Input\" => {\n                        let clean = value.trim_start_matches('$').trim_end_matches(\"/1K\");\n                        if let Ok(v) = clean.parse::<f64>() {\n                            self.forge_config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_input = v;\n                            return true;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    \"Sonnet Cost/Output\" => {\n                        let clean = value.trim_start_matches('$').trim_end_matches(\"/1K\");\n                        if let Ok(v) = clean.parse::<f64>() {\n                            self.forge_config.cost_tracking.sonnet_cost_per_1k_output = v;\n                            return true;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    _ => {}\n                }\n            }\n            ConfigMenuType::Worker => {\n                match label {\n                    \"Max Workers\" => {\n                        if let Ok(v) = value.parse::<u64>() {\n                            self.forge_config.workers.max_workers = v.max(1);\n                            return true;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    \"Default Model\" => {\n                        self.forge_config.workers.default_model = value.to_lowercase();\n                        return true;\n                    }\n                    \"Worker Timeout\" => {\n                        if let Ok(v) = value.trim_end_matches(\" min\").parse::<i64>() {\n                            self.forge_config.auto_recovery.stuck_task_timeout_mins = v;\n                            return true;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    \"Auto-Recovery\" => {\n                        self.forge_config.auto_recovery.enabled = value == \"enabled\";\n                        return true;\n                    }\n                    \"Dead Worker Policy\" => {\n                        self.forge_config.auto_recovery.dead_worker_policy = value.to_string();\n                        return true;\n                    }\n                    \"Max Restart Attempts\" => {\n                        if let Ok(v) = value.parse::<u8>() {\n                            self.forge_config.auto_recovery.max_restart_attempts = v;\n                            return true;\n                        }\n                    }\n                    _ => {}\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        false\n    }\n\n    /// Check if an update is available by comparing source binary timestamp.\n    fn check_for_update(&mut self) {\n        use std::env;\n        use std::fs;\n\n        // Check every 10 seconds\n        if self.last_update_check.elapsed() < Duration::from_secs(10) {\n            return;\n        }\n        self.last_update_check = Instant::now();\n\n        let forge_src = env::var(\"FORGE_SRC\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"/home/coder/forge\".to_string());\n\n        let source_binary = format!(\"{}/target/release/forge\", forge_src);\n        let installed_binary = env::current_exe()\n            .ok()\n            .or_else(|| {\n                env::var(\"HOME\")\n                    .ok()\n                    .map(|h| format!(\"{}/.cargo/bin/forge\", h).into())\n            })\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| \"forge\".into());\n\n        // Compare modification times\n        if let (Ok(source_meta), Ok(installed_meta)) = (\n            fs::metadata(&source_binary),\n            fs::metadata(&installed_binary),\n        ) {\n            if let (Ok(source_time), Ok(installed_time)) =\n                (source_meta.modified(), installed_meta.modified())\n            {\n                self.update_available = source_time > installed_time;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Trigger forge self-update from GitHub releases (non-blocking).\n    ///\n    /// Spawns an async task to:\n    /// 1. Check GitHub releases for latest version\n    /// 2. Download the new binary if update available\n    /// 3. Perform atomic swap\n    ///\n    /// Shows immediate visual feedback via the update_progress overlay.\n    #[cfg(feature = \"self-update\")]\n    fn trigger_update(&mut self) {\n        use std::thread;\n\n        // Prevent multiple concurrent updates\n        if self.update_in_progress {\n            self.status_message = Some(\"Update already in progress...\".to_string());\n            self.mark_dirty();\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Show immediate visual feedback\n        self.update_in_progress = true;\n        self.update_progress = UpdateProgress {\n            status: \"Checking for updates...\".to_string(),\n            percent: 0,\n            in_progress: true,\n        };\n        self.mark_dirty();\n\n        // Get current version\n        let current_version = env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\").to_string();\n\n        // Create channel for receiving result\n        let (tx_result, rx_result) = mpsc::channel();\n        self.update_result_rx = Some(rx_result);\n\n        // Spawn background thread with tokio runtime for async operations\n        thread::spawn(move || {\n            // Create a tokio runtime for this thread\n            let rt = match tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n                .enable_all()\n                .build()\n            {\n                Ok(rt) => rt,\n                Err(e) => {\n                    let _ = tx_result.send(UpdateResult::Failed(format!(\n                        \"Failed to create runtime: {}\",\n                        e\n                    )));\n                    return;\n                }\n            };\n\n            rt.block_on(async {\n                use forge_core::self_update::{check_for_update, perform_update, UpdateStatus};\n\n                // Check for updates\n                let status = match check_for_update(&current_version).await {\n                    Ok(status) => status,\n                    Err(e) => {\n                        let _ = tx_result.send(UpdateResult::Failed(format!(\n                            \"Failed to check for updates: {}\",\n                            e\n                        )));\n                        return;\n                    }\n                };\n\n                match status {\n                    UpdateStatus::UpToDate => {\n                        let _ = tx_result.send(UpdateResult::AlreadyUpToDate);\n                    }\n                    UpdateStatus::Available {\n                        current: _,\n                        latest: _,\n                        download_url,\n                        asset_size,\n                    } => {\n                        // Perform the update without progress callback (download is typically fast)\n                        let result = perform_update(&download_url, asset_size, None).await;\n\n                        match result {\n                            Ok(forge_core::self_update::UpdateResult::Success { old_version, new_version }) => {\n                                let _ = tx_result.send(UpdateResult::Success {\n                                    old_version,\n                                    new_version,  // Use the version from the result\n                                });\n                            }\n                            Ok(forge_core::self_update::UpdateResult::AlreadyUpToDate) => {\n                                let _ = tx_result.send(UpdateResult::AlreadyUpToDate);\n                            }\n                            Ok(forge_core::self_update::UpdateResult::Failed(err)) => {\n                                let _ = tx_result.send(UpdateResult::Failed(err));\n                            }\n                            Err(e) => {\n                                let _ = tx_result.send(UpdateResult::Failed(format!(\n                                    \"Update failed: {}\",\n                                    e\n                                )));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            });\n        });\n    }\n\n    /// Trigger forge update fallback (when self-update feature is disabled).\n    #[cfg(not(feature = \"self-update\"))]\n    fn trigger_update(&mut self) {\n        self.status_message = Some(\"Self-update not available in this build.\".to_string());\n        self.mark_dirty();\n    }\n\n    /// Poll for update completion and progress (called from event loop).\n    fn poll_update_result(&mut self) {\n        if let Some(ref rx) = self.update_result_rx {\n            // Non-blocking check for result\n            if let Ok(result) = rx.try_recv() {\n                self.update_in_progress = false;\n                self.update_progress.in_progress = false;\n                self.update_result_rx = None;\n\n                match result {\n                    UpdateResult::Success {\n                        old_version,\n                        new_version,\n                    } => {\n                        self.status_message = Some(format!(\n                            \"Updated forge v{} -> v{}! Restarting...\",\n                            old_version, new_version\n                        ));\n                        self.update_available = false;\n                        self.update_progress.percent = 100;\n                        self.update_ready_for_restart = true;\n                        self.should_quit = true;\n                    }\n                    UpdateResult::AlreadyUpToDate => {\n                        self.status_message =\n                            Some(format!(\"Already running latest version v{}\", env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\")));\n                    }\n                    UpdateResult::Failed(err) => {\n                        self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Update failed: {}\", err));\n                    }\n                }\n                self.mark_dirty();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Run the main application loop.\n    pub fn run(&mut self) -> AppResult<()> {\n        // Setup terminal\n        crossterm::terminal::enable_raw_mode()?;\n        let mut stdout = io::stdout();\n        crossterm::execute!(\n            stdout,\n            crossterm::terminal::EnterAlternateScreen,\n            crossterm::event::EnableMouseCapture\n        )?;\n        let backend = CrosstermBackend::new(stdout);\n        let mut terminal = Terminal::new(backend)?;\n\n        // Main loop\n        let result = self.run_inner(&mut terminal);\n\n        // Restore terminal\n        crossterm::terminal::disable_raw_mode()?;\n        crossterm::execute!(\n            terminal.backend_mut(),\n            crossterm::terminal::LeaveAlternateScreen,\n            crossterm::event::DisableMouseCapture\n        )?;\n        terminal.show_cursor()?;\n\n        // If an update was downloaded, restart with the new binary\n        // This replaces the current process via exec() - does not return on success\n        #[cfg(feature = \"self-update\")]\n        if self.update_ready_for_restart {\n            use tracing::info;\n            info!(\"Update ready, restarting with new binary...\");\n            eprintln!(\"\\n🔄 Restarting forge with the new version...\\n\");\n\n            match forge_core::restart_with_new_binary() {\n                Ok(()) => {\n                    // This should not be reached - exec() replaces the process\n                    unreachable!(\"exec() returned, which should never happen\");\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    eprintln!(\"❌ Failed to restart with new binary: {}\", e);\n                    eprintln!(\"You may need to manually restart forge.\");\n                    // Return the original result since restart failed\n                    return result;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        result\n    }\n\n    /// Run the TUI in client mode, connecting to a FORGE server.\n    ///\n    /// This method starts the TUI application in client mode, where it connects\n    /// to a remote FORGE server via WebSocket for multi-user collaboration.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `config` - Client configuration with server URL and credentials\n    pub fn run_with_client(config: ClientConfig) -> AppResult<()> {\n        use std::sync::mpsc::{self, Receiver, Sender, TryRecvError};\n        use tokio::runtime::Runtime;\n\n        info!(\"Starting FORGE in client mode, connecting to {}\", config.server_url);\n\n        // Create tokio runtime for async WebSocket client\n        let rt = Runtime::new()\n            .map_err(|e| Box::new(std::io::Error::new(std::io::ErrorKind::Other, e)))?;\n\n        // Create channels for communication with the background client task\n        let (request_tx, request_rx) = mpsc::channel::<ServerClientRequest>();\n        let (response_tx, response_rx) = mpsc::channel::<ServerClientMessage>();\n\n        // Clone config for the background task\n        let server_url = config.server_url.clone();\n        let user_id = config.user_id.clone();\n        let password = config.password.clone();\n\n        // Spawn background task for WebSocket client\n        rt.spawn(async move {\n            Self::run_client_background_task(server_url, user_id, password, request_rx, response_tx).await;\n        });\n\n        // Create app with client mode enabled\n        let mut app = Self::new();\n        app.server_client_tx = Some(request_tx);\n        app.server_client_rx = Some(response_rx);\n        app.server_url = Some(config.server_url.clone());\n\n        // Setup terminal\n        crossterm::terminal::enable_raw_mode()?;\n        let mut stdout = io::stdout();\n        crossterm::execute!(\n            stdout,\n            crossterm::terminal::EnterAlternateScreen,\n            crossterm::event::EnableMouseCapture\n        )?;\n        let backend = CrosstermBackend::new(stdout);\n        let mut terminal = Terminal::new(backend)?;\n\n        // Show connecting message\n        app.status_message = Some(format!(\"Connecting to {}...\", config.server_url));\n        app.mark_dirty();\n\n        // Main loop with server client polling\n        let result = app.run_loop_with_client(&mut terminal);\n\n        // Restore terminal\n        crossterm::terminal::disable_raw_mode()?;\n        crossterm::execute!(\n            terminal.backend_mut(),\n            crossterm::terminal::LeaveAlternateScreen,\n            crossterm::event::DisableMouseCapture\n        )?;\n        terminal.show_cursor()?;\n\n        result\n    }\n\n    /// Background task that runs the WebSocket client.\n    ///\n    /// This task handles all WebSocket communication with the FORGE server,\n    /// including authentication, receiving state updates, and sending requests.\n    async fn run_client_background_task(\n        server_url: String,\n        user_id: String,\n        password: String,\n        request_rx: Receiver<ServerClientRequest>,\n        response_tx: Sender<ServerClientMessage>,\n    ) {\n        use forge_server::client::ForgeClient;\n        use forge_server::protocol::{ClientMessage, ServerMessage};\n        use tokio::sync::mpsc;\n\n        info!(\"Client background task: connecting to {}\", server_url);\n\n        // Create async channels for tokio task\n        let (async_req_tx, mut async_req_rx) = mpsc::channel::<ServerClientRequest>(100);\n\n        // Forward sync requests to async channel\n        thread::spawn(move || {\n            while let Ok(req) = request_rx.recv() {\n                if async_req_tx.blocking_send(req).is_err() {\n                    warn!(\"Failed to forward request to async task\");\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        // Clone server_url for later use (since it's moved into ClientConfig)\n        let server_url_clone = server_url.clone();\n\n        // Create client\n        let client = ForgeClient::new(forge_server::client::ClientConfig {\n            server_url,\n            user_id,\n            password,\n        });\n\n        // Subscribe to state updates BEFORE connecting\n        let mut state_rx = client.subscribe();\n\n        // Spawn connection task (client.connect_and_run no longer consumes self)\n        let client_clone = client.clone();\n        let response_tx_clone = response_tx.clone();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            if let Err(e) = client_clone.connect_and_run().await {\n                let _ = response_tx_clone.send(ServerClientMessage::ConnectionError {\n                    message: e.to_string(),\n                });\n            } else {\n                let _ = response_tx_clone.send(ServerClientMessage::Disconnected);\n            }\n        });\n\n        // Task to forward state updates from server to TUI\n        let response_tx_state = response_tx.clone();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            while let Ok(server_msg) = state_rx.recv().await {\n                let tui_msg = match server_msg {\n                    ServerMessage::Welcome { session, server_info: _ } => {\n                        ServerClientMessage::Connected {\n                            session,\n                            server_url: server_url_clone.clone(),\n                        }\n                    }\n                    ServerMessage::StateUpdate(update) => {\n                        ServerClientMessage::StateUpdate {\n                            workers: update.workers,\n                            beads: update.beads,\n                        }\n                    }\n                    ServerMessage::UserJoined { user, display_name, role } => {\n                        ServerClientMessage::UserJoined { user, display_name, role }\n                    }\n                    ServerMessage::UserLeft { user } => {\n                        ServerClientMessage::UserLeft { user }\n                    }\n                    ServerMessage::BeadAssigned { bead_id, assigned_to, assigned_by } => {\n                        ServerClientMessage::BeadAssigned { bead_id, assigned_to, assigned_by }\n                    }\n                    ServerMessage::ChatMessage { from, message, timestamp } => {\n                        ServerClientMessage::ChatMessage { from, message, timestamp }\n                    }\n                    ServerMessage::Error { message } => {\n                        ServerClientMessage::ConnectionError { message }\n                    }\n                    ServerMessage::Ping | ServerMessage::WorkerChanged { .. } | ServerMessage::BeadChanged { .. } => {\n                        // These are handled internally or don't need TUI notification\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                };\n\n                if response_tx_state.send(tui_msg).is_err() {\n                    warn!(\"Failed to send message to TUI\");\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        // Handle incoming requests from TUI\n        while let Some(req) = async_req_rx.recv().await {\n            let client_msg = match req {\n                ServerClientRequest::AssignBead { bead_id, to } => {\n                    Some(ClientMessage::AssignBead { bead_id, to })\n                }\n                ServerClientRequest::UnassignBead { bead_id } => {\n                    Some(ClientMessage::UnassignBead { bead_id })\n                }\n                ServerClientRequest::SendChat { message } => {\n                    Some(ClientMessage::ChatMessage { message })\n                }\n                ServerClientRequest::UpdateView { view } => {\n                    Some(ClientMessage::UpdateView { view })\n                }\n                ServerClientRequest::SpawnWorker { model, count } => {\n                    Some(ClientMessage::SpawnWorker { model, count })\n                }\n                ServerClientRequest::KillWorker { worker_id } => {\n                    Some(ClientMessage::KillWorker { worker_id })\n                }\n            };\n\n            if let Some(msg) = client_msg {\n                // Use send_direct to send the message (works because connect_and_run no longer consumes self)\n                if let Err(e) = client.send_direct(msg).await {\n                    warn!(\"Failed to send message to server: {}\", e);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// The inner event loop with frame-rate limiting and server client support.\n    fn run_loop_with_client(&mut self, terminal: &mut Terminal<CrosstermBackend<io::Stdout>>) -> AppResult<()> {\n        // Memory update interval (every 60 frames = ~1 second at 60fps)\n        let mut memory_update_counter: u32 = 0;\n        const MEMORY_UPDATE_INTERVAL: u32 = 60;\n\n        while !self.should_quit {\n            let frame_start = Instant::now();\n\n            // Advance spinner animation when chat is pending\n            if self.chat_pending {\n                self.chat_spinner_frame = (self.chat_spinner_frame + 1) % 4;\n                self.mark_dirty(); // Ensure UI updates for spinner animation\n            }\n\n            // Poll for server client messages FIRST - in client mode, this is critical\n            self.poll_server_client_messages();\n\n            // Poll for chat responses - this is non-blocking and must happen\n            // frequently to ensure responsive chat UX\n            self.poll_chat_responses();\n\n            // Update streaming display if active\n            self.update_streaming();\n\n            // Poll for config hot-reload changes\n            self.poll_config_changes();\n\n            // Optimized data polling - only poll if interval elapsed\n            let needs_poll = self.last_poll_time.elapsed() >= self.data_poll_interval;\n            if needs_poll {\n                let data_changed = self.data_manager.poll_updates();\n                self.last_poll_time = Instant::now();\n\n                // Mark dirty if data actually changed\n                if data_changed {\n                    self.mark_dirty();\n                }\n\n                // Check for pending bell notifications and ring if needed\n                if self.data_manager.take_pending_bell() {\n                    crate::alert::AlertNotifier::ring_bell();\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Poll for chat responses again after data polling\n            self.poll_chat_responses();\n\n            // Update streaming display if active\n            self.update_streaming();\n\n            // Poll for update completion\n            self.poll_update_result();\n\n            // Check for updates periodically\n            self.check_for_update();\n\n            // Only draw if dirty or at minimum rate (timestamp updates every second)\n            let needs_redraw = self.take_dirty()\n                || self.last_timestamp_update.elapsed() >= TIMESTAMP_CACHE_DURATION;\n\n            // Measure render time\n            let render_start = Instant::now();\n            if needs_redraw {\n                terminal.draw(|frame| self.draw(frame))?;\n            }\n            let render_us = render_start.elapsed().as_micros() as u64;\n\n            // Calculate remaining time in frame for event handling\n            let elapsed = frame_start.elapsed();\n            let timeout_for_events = if elapsed < FRAME_DURATION {\n                FRAME_DURATION - elapsed\n            } else {\n                Duration::ZERO\n            };\n\n            // Handle events with adaptive timeout\n            let event_timeout = if timeout_for_events > Duration::ZERO {\n                timeout_for_events\n            } else {\n                // If frame took too long, use shorter timeout\n                Duration::from_millis(10)\n            };\n\n            if event::poll(event_timeout)? {\n                if let Event::Key(key) = event::read()? {\n                    self.data_manager.record_event();\n                    self.handle_key_event(key);\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Calculate event loop time (everything except render)\n            let frame_elapsed = frame_start.elapsed();\n            let event_loop_us = frame_elapsed.as_micros() as u64 - render_us;\n\n            // Record frame performance metrics\n            self.data_manager.record_frame_perf(event_loop_us, render_us);\n\n            // Periodically update memory usage\n            memory_update_counter += 1;\n            if memory_update_counter >= MEMORY_UPDATE_INTERVAL {\n                self.data_manager.update_memory();\n                self.data_manager.prune_perf_alerts();\n                memory_update_counter = 0;\n            }\n\n            // Frame-rate limiting: sleep if frame was too fast\n            if frame_elapsed < FRAME_DURATION {\n                let sleep_time = FRAME_DURATION - frame_elapsed;\n                std::thread::sleep(sleep_time);\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Poll for messages from the server client.\n    fn poll_server_client_messages(&mut self) {\n        // Take the receiver temporarily to avoid borrow checker issues\n        let rx = self.server_client_rx.take();\n        if let Some(rx) = rx {\n            loop {\n                match rx.try_recv() {\n                    Ok(msg) => {\n                        self.handle_server_client_message(msg);\n                        self.mark_dirty();\n                    }\n                    Err(TryRecvError::Empty) => break,\n                    Err(TryRecvError::Disconnected) => {\n                        self.is_server_connected = false;\n                        self.status_message = Some(\"Disconnected from server\".to_string());\n                        break;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            // Put the receiver back\n            self.server_client_rx = Some(rx);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a message from the server client.\n    fn handle_server_client_message(&mut self, msg: ServerClientMessage) {\n        match msg {\n            ServerClientMessage::Connected { session, server_url } => {\n                self.is_server_connected = true;\n                self.user_session = Some(session.clone());\n                self.server_url = Some(server_url.clone());\n                self.status_message = Some(format!(\n                    \"Connected to {} as {} ({})\",\n                    server_url, session.display_name, session.role\n                ));\n            }\n            ServerClientMessage::StateUpdate { workers, beads } => {\n                // Update data manager with server state\n                self.data_manager.update_from_server_state(workers, beads);\n            }\n            ServerClientMessage::UserJoined { user, display_name, role } => {\n                self.sessions_panel.add_user(user, display_name.clone(), role);\n                self.status_message = Some(format!(\"{} ({}) joined\", display_name, role));\n            }\n            ServerClientMessage::UserLeft { user } => {\n                self.sessions_panel.remove_user(&user);\n                self.status_message = Some(format!(\"{} left\", user));\n            }\n            ServerClientMessage::BeadAssigned { bead_id, assigned_to, assigned_by } => {\n                self.status_message = Some(format!(\n                    \"Bead {} assigned to {} by {}\",\n                    bead_id, assigned_to, assigned_by\n                ));\n            }\n            ServerClientMessage::ChatMessage { from, message, timestamp: _ } => {\n                // Add chat message from another user\n                self.chat_history.push(ChatExchange {\n                    user_query: format!(\"[{}] {}\", from, message),\n                    assistant_response: String::new(),\n                    timestamp: chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),\n                    is_error: false,\n                    tool_calls: Vec::new(),\n                    side_effects: Vec::new(),\n                    confirmation: None,\n                    metadata: ResponseMetadata::default(),\n                    error_guidance: None,\n                });\n\n                // Keep only last 10 exchanges\n                if self.chat_history.len() > 10 {\n                    self.chat_history.remove(0);\n                }\n            }\n            ServerClientMessage::ConnectionError { message } => {\n                self.is_server_connected = false;\n                self.status_message = Some(format!(\"Connection error: {}\", message));\n            }\n            ServerClientMessage::Disconnected => {\n                self.is_server_connected = false;\n                self.status_message = Some(\"Disconnected from server\".to_string());\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Send a request to the server client.\n    fn send_server_client_request(&self, request: ServerClientRequest) {\n        if let Some(ref tx) = self.server_client_tx {\n            let _ = tx.send(request);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// The inner event loop with frame-rate limiting and optimized polling.\n    fn run_inner(&mut self, terminal: &mut Terminal<CrosstermBackend<io::Stdout>>) -> AppResult<()> {\n        // Memory update interval (every 60 frames = ~1 second at 60fps)\n        let mut memory_update_counter: u32 = 0;\n        const MEMORY_UPDATE_INTERVAL: u32 = 60;\n\n        while !self.should_quit {\n            let frame_start = Instant::now();\n\n            // Advance spinner animation when chat is pending\n            if self.chat_pending {\n                self.chat_spinner_frame = (self.chat_spinner_frame + 1) % 4;\n                self.mark_dirty(); // Ensure UI updates for spinner animation\n            }\n\n            // Poll for server client messages FIRST when in server/client mode\n            // This is critical for multi-user collaboration and state synchronization\n            if self.server_client_rx.is_some() {\n                self.poll_server_client_messages();\n            }\n\n            // Poll for chat responses FIRST - this is non-blocking and must happen\n            // frequently to ensure responsive chat UX. Don't let data polling block it.\n            self.poll_chat_responses();\n\n            // Update streaming display if active\n            self.update_streaming();\n\n            // Poll for config hot-reload changes\n            self.poll_config_changes();\n\n            // Optimized data polling - only poll if interval elapsed\n            let needs_poll = self.last_poll_time.elapsed() >= self.data_poll_interval;\n            if needs_poll {\n                let data_changed = self.data_manager.poll_updates();\n                self.last_poll_time = Instant::now();\n\n                // Mark dirty if data actually changed\n                if data_changed {\n                    self.mark_dirty();\n                }\n\n                // Check for pending bell notifications and ring if needed\n                if self.data_manager.take_pending_bell() {\n                    crate::alert::AlertNotifier::ring_bell();\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Poll for chat responses again after data polling in case a response\n            // arrived while we were doing the (potentially slow) data poll\n            self.poll_chat_responses();\n\n            // Update streaming display if active\n            self.update_streaming();\n\n            // Poll for update completion\n            self.poll_update_result();\n\n            // Check for updates periodically\n            self.check_for_update();\n\n            // Only draw if dirty or at minimum rate (timestamp updates every second)\n            let needs_redraw = self.take_dirty()\n                || self.last_timestamp_update.elapsed() >= TIMESTAMP_CACHE_DURATION;\n\n            // Measure render time\n            let render_start = Instant::now();\n            if needs_redraw {\n                terminal.draw(|frame| self.draw(frame))?;\n            }\n            let render_us = render_start.elapsed().as_micros() as u64;\n\n            // Calculate remaining time in frame for event handling\n            let elapsed = frame_start.elapsed();\n            let timeout_for_events = if elapsed < FRAME_DURATION {\n                FRAME_DURATION - elapsed\n            } else {\n                Duration::ZERO\n            };\n\n            // Handle events with adaptive timeout\n            let event_timeout = if timeout_for_events > Duration::ZERO {\n                timeout_for_events\n            } else {\n                // If frame took too long, use shorter timeout\n                Duration::from_millis(10)\n            };\n\n            if event::poll(event_timeout)? {\n                if let Event::Key(key) = event::read()? {\n                    self.data_manager.record_event();\n                    self.handle_key_event(key);\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Calculate event loop time (everything except render)\n            let frame_elapsed = frame_start.elapsed();\n            let event_loop_us = frame_elapsed.as_micros() as u64 - render_us;\n\n            // Record frame performance metrics\n            self.data_manager.record_frame_perf(event_loop_us, render_us);\n\n            // Periodically update memory usage\n            memory_update_counter += 1;\n            if memory_update_counter >= MEMORY_UPDATE_INTERVAL {\n                self.data_manager.update_memory();\n                self.data_manager.prune_perf_alerts();\n                memory_update_counter = 0;\n            }\n\n            // Frame-rate limiting: sleep if frame was too fast\n            if frame_elapsed < FRAME_DURATION {\n                let sleep_time = FRAME_DURATION - frame_elapsed;\n                std::thread::sleep(sleep_time);\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Draw the UI.\n    pub fn draw(&mut self, frame: &mut Frame) {\n        let area = frame.area();\n\n        // Update cached size if changed\n        if self.size_changed(area) {\n            self.update_cached_size(area);\n        }\n\n        // Main layout: header, content, footer\n        let chunks = Layout::default()\n            .direction(Direction::Vertical)\n            .constraints([\n                Constraint::Length(3), // Header\n                Constraint::Min(10),   // Content\n                Constraint::Length(2), // Footer\n            ])\n            .split(area);\n\n        self.draw_header(frame, chunks[0]);\n        self.draw_content(frame, chunks[1]);\n        self.draw_footer(frame, chunks[2]);\n\n        // Draw help overlay if active\n        if self.show_help {\n            self.draw_help_overlay(frame, area);\n        }\n\n        // Draw kill worker dialog if active\n        if self.show_kill_dialog {\n            self.draw_kill_dialog(frame, area);\n        }\n\n        // Draw confirmation dialog if active\n        if self.show_confirmation {\n            self.draw_confirmation_dialog(frame, area);\n        }\n\n        // Draw config menu overlay if active\n        if self.show_config_menu {\n            if let Some(menu_type) = self.config_menu_type {\n                let items = match menu_type {\n                    ConfigMenuType::Settings => build_settings_items(&self.forge_config),\n                    ConfigMenuType::Budget => build_budget_items(&self.forge_config),\n                    ConfigMenuType::Worker => build_worker_items(&self.forge_config),\n                };\n                let theme = self.theme_manager.current();\n                draw_config_menu(\n                    frame,\n                    crate::config_menu::ConfigMenuParams {\n                        area,\n                        menu_type,\n                        items: &items,\n                        selected: self.config_menu_selected,\n                        editing: self.config_menu_editing,\n                        edit_buffer: &self.config_menu_input,\n                        theme,\n                    },\n                );\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Draw task detail overlay if active\n        if self.show_task_detail {\n            self.draw_task_detail_overlay(frame, area);\n        }\n\n        // Draw alert detail overlay if active\n        if self.show_alert_detail {\n            self.draw_alert_detail_overlay(frame, area);\n        }\n\n        // Draw update notification banner if update available\n        if self.update_available {\n            self.draw_update_banner(frame, area);\n        }\n\n        // Draw network unreachable banner if network is down\n        if !self.network_available {\n            self.draw_network_banner(frame, area);\n        }\n\n        // Draw status message overlay if present\n        if let Some(ref msg) = self.status_message {\n            self.draw_status_message(frame, area, msg);\n        }\n\n        // Draw update in progress overlay (higher priority than status message)\n        if self.update_in_progress {\n            self.draw_update_progress_overlay(frame, area);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Draw the header bar with cached timestamp.\n    fn draw_header(&mut self, frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect) {\n        let now = self.get_cached_timestamp();\n        let theme = self.theme_manager.current();\n        let version = env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\");\n        let title = format!(\" FORGE v{} - {} \", version, self.current_view.title());\n        let title_len = title.len();\n\n        // Terminal dimensions display\n        let dimensions = format!(\"{}×{}\", frame.area().width, frame.area().height);\n\n        // Get alert badge for display\n        let alert_badge = self.data_manager.alert_badge();\n        let alert_text = if alert_badge.should_display() {\n            format!(\" {} \", alert_badge.format_compact())\n        } else {\n            String::new()\n        };\n        let alert_color = if alert_badge.critical > 0 {\n            theme.colors.status_error\n        } else {\n            theme.colors.status_warning\n        };\n\n        // Get unacknowledged errors for display\n        let unacknowledged = self.error_recovery.unacknowledged_errors();\n        let error_text = if !unacknowledged.is_empty() {\n            // Show count of unacknowledged errors\n            let count = unacknowledged.len();\n            format!(\"⚠️{} \", count)\n        } else {\n            String::new()\n        };\n\n        // Determine system status from real data\n        let paused_count = self.paused_workers.len();\n        let (status_text, status_color) = if let Some(err) = self.data_manager.init_error() {\n            (\n                format!(\"[Error: {}]\", truncate_status_error(err)),\n                theme.colors.status_error,\n            )\n        } else if !self.data_manager.is_ready() {\n            (\"[Loading...]\".to_string(), theme.colors.status_warning)\n        } else {\n            let counts = self.data_manager.worker_counts();\n            if counts.unhealthy() > 0 {\n                let paused_suffix = if paused_count > 0 {\n                    format!(\" ⏸{}\", paused_count)\n                } else {\n                    String::new()\n                };\n                (\n                    format!(\"[{} unhealthy{}]\", counts.unhealthy(), paused_suffix),\n                    theme.colors.status_warning,\n                )\n            } else if counts.total == 0 {\n                (\"[No workers]\".to_string(), theme.colors.text_dim)\n            } else if paused_count > 0 {\n                // Show paused count when there are paused workers\n                (\n                    format!(\"[{} workers ⏸{}]\", counts.total, paused_count),\n                    Color::Magenta,\n                )\n            } else {\n                (\n                    format!(\"[{} workers]\", counts.total),\n                    theme.colors.status_healthy,\n                )\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Calculate spacing to right-align timestamp, dimensions, alert badge, error indicator, and status\n        let right_content_len = now.len() + 2 + dimensions.len() + 2 + alert_text.len() + error_text.len() + status_text.len();\n        let spacing = area\n            .width\n            .saturating_sub(title_len as u16 + right_content_len as u16 + 2)\n            as usize;\n\n        let mut header_spans = vec![\n            Span::styled(\n                title,\n                Style::default()\n                    .fg(theme.colors.header)\n                    .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),\n            ),\n            Span::raw(\" \".repeat(spacing)),\n            Span::styled(now, Style::default().fg(theme.colors.text_dim)),\n            Span::raw(\"  \"),\n            Span::styled(dimensions, Style::default().fg(theme.colors.text_dim)),\n            Span::raw(\"  \"),\n        ];\n\n        // Add error indicator if there are unacknowledged errors\n        if !error_text.is_empty() {\n            header_spans.push(Span::styled(&error_text, Style::default().fg(theme.colors.status_error).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)));\n        }\n\n        // Add alert badge if there are alerts\n        if alert_badge.should_display() {\n            header_spans.push(Span::styled(&alert_text, Style::default().fg(alert_color).add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)));\n        }\n\n        header_spans.push(Span::styled(status_text, Style::default().fg(status_color)));\n\n        let header = Paragraph::new(Line::from(header_spans))\n        .block(\n            Block::default()\n                .borders(Borders::ALL)\n                .border_style(Style::default().fg(theme.colors.border_dim)),\n        );\n\n        frame.render_widget(header, area);\n    }\n\n    /// Draw the main content area based on current view.\n    fn draw_content(&mut self, frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect) {\n        match self.current_view {\n            View::Overview => self.draw_overview(frame, area),\n            View::Workers => self.draw_workers(frame, area),\n            View::Tasks => self.draw_tasks(frame, area),\n            View::Costs => self.draw_costs(frame, area),\n            View::Metrics => self.draw_metrics(frame, area),\n            View::Perf => self.draw_perf(frame, area),\n            View::Logs => self.draw_logs(frame, area),\n            View::Subscriptions => self.draw_subscriptions(frame, area),\n            View::Alerts => self.draw_alerts(frame, area),\n            View::Routing => self.draw_routing(frame, area),\n            View::Chat => self.draw_chat(frame, area),\n            View::Sessions => self.draw_sessions(frame, area),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Draw the footer with hotkey hints.\n    fn draw_footer(&self, frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect) {\n        let theme = self.theme_manager.current();\n        let hotkey_style = Style::default().fg(theme.colors.hotkey);\n        let hints = vec![\n            Span::styled(\"[o]\", hotkey_style),\n            Span::raw(\"Overview \"),\n            Span::styled(\"[w]\", hotkey_style),\n            Span::raw(\"Workers \"),\n            Span::styled(\"[t]\", hotkey_style),\n            Span::raw(\"Tasks \"),\n            Span::styled(\"[c]\", hotkey_style),\n            Span::raw(\"Costs \"),\n            Span::styled(\"[m]\", hotkey_style),\n            Span::raw(\"Metrics \"),\n            Span::styled(\"[p]\", hotkey_style),\n            Span::raw(\"Perf \"),\n            Span::styled(\"[l]\", hotkey_style),\n            Span::raw(\"Logs \"),\n            Span::styled(\"[u]\", hotkey_style),\n            Span::raw(\"Subs \"),\n            Span::styled(\"[a]\", hotkey_style),\n            Span::raw(\"Alerts \"),\n            Span::styled(\"[r]\", hotkey_style),\n            Span::raw(\"Route \"),\n            Span::styled(\"[:]\", hotkey_style),\n            Span::raw(\"Chat \"),\n            Span::styled(\"[?]\", hotkey_style),\n            Span::raw(\"Help \"),\n            Span::styled(\"[C]\", hotkey_style),\n            Span::raw(\"Theme \"),\n            Span::styled(\"[q]\", hotkey_style),\n            Span::raw(\"Quit\"),\n        ];\n\n        let dims_text = format!(\"{}x{}\", area.width, area.height);\n\n        let footer = Paragraph::new(Line::from(hints))\n            .style(Style::default().fg(theme.colors.text_dim))\n            .block(\n                Block::default()\n                    .borders(Borders::TOP)\n                    .title(Span::styled(\n                        dims_text,\n                        Style::default().fg(theme.colors.border_dim),\n                    ))\n                    .title_alignment(ratatui::layout::Alignment::Right),\n            );\n\n        frame.render_widget(footer, area);\n    }\n\n    /// Draw the Overview/Dashboard view with cached layout mode.\n    ///\n    /// Layout adapts based on terminal width:\n    /// - Ultra-wide (199+): 3-column layout with all 6
